revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 登彌神社 |画像 = |所在地 = {{JPN}} [[奈良県]][[奈良市]]石木町648-1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|39|6.5|N|135|44|53|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |緯度度 = |緯度分 = |緯度秒 = |経度度 = |経度分 = |経度秒 = |祭神 = [[神皇産霊神]][[櫛玉饒速日命]][[天児屋命]][[高皇産霊神]][[誉田別命]] |神体 = |社格 = [[式内社]](旧[[県社]]) |創建 = |本殿 = [[春日造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 十月八日、九 |神事 = }} '''登彌神社'''（とみじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[奈良市]]石木町に[[鎮座]]する[[式内社]]で、旧社格は、[[県社]]
*筒男。 由緒については、[[皇紀]]４年春２月23日、[[神武天皇]]がこの地に於いて皇祖天神を祭祀されたのが、淵源であり、その後、登美連が祖先である[[饒速日命]]の居住地であったこの地に命の夫妻を奉祀したのが神社の創建とされている。
== 祭神 == 本殿が、南向きで西本殿と東本殿が横並びとなっており、西本殿の祭神が[[神皇産霊神]]・饒速日命・[[天児屋命]]、東本殿の祭神が[[高皇産霊神]]・[[誉田別命]]。
== 文化財 == *文化庁より、令和２年４月３日、本殿・拝殿・神饌所・手水舎・社務所が登録有形文化財に登録される。 *[[粥占い]]が、昭和57年３月１日、奈良市無形民俗文化財に指定される。 == 境内神社 == *祓殿神社(祭神：[[瀬織津比売|瀬織津比売神]]・[[速秋津比売神]]・[[気吹戸主神]]・[[速佐須良比売神]]・[[表筒男神]]・[[中筒男神]]・[[底筒男神]]) *山室神社(祭神:[[大物主神]]・[[菅原道真公]]) *豊穂神社(祭神：[[大日孁|大日孁命]]・[[豊受比売|豊受比売神]]・天宇受女神) *荒神神社(祭神:[[大山祇神]]・庭高津日神) *比良田神社(祭神:[[猿田彦神]]・[[大己貴神]]・[[八重事代主神]])
== アクセス == *[[近鉄橿原線]]、[[九条駅 (奈良県)|九条駅]]より西へ２km(徒歩20分)
*駐車場あり {{脚注ヘルプ}} *
<!-- == 外部リンク == --> {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やたにますくしたまひこしんしや}} [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧県社]] [[Category:大和郡山市の歴史]] [[Category:大和郡山市の建築物]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大やたにますくしたまひこしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の重要文化財]]

{{出典の明記|date=2024年5月23日 (木) 23:58 (UTC)}} {{神社 |名称 = 登彌神社 |画像 = |所在地 = {{JPN}} [[奈良県]][[奈良市]]石木町648-1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|39|38|N|135|45|57|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |緯度度 = |緯度分 = |緯度秒 = |経度度 = |経度分 = |経度秒 = |祭神 = [[神皇産霊神]][[櫛玉饒速日命]][[天児屋命]][[高皇産霊神]][[誉田別命]] |神体 = |社格 = [[式内社]](旧[[県社]]) |創建 = |本殿 = [[春日造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 十月八日、九 |神事 = }} '''登彌神社'''（とみじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[奈良市]]石木町に[[鎮座]]する[[式内社]]で、旧社格は、[[県社]]。
由緒については、[[皇紀]]４年春２月23日、[[神武天皇]]がこの地に於いて皇祖天神を祭祀されたのが、淵源であり、その後、登美連が祖先である[[饒速日命]]の居住地であったこの地に命の夫妻を奉祀したのが神社の創建とされている。小宮も合わせて二十二柱の神を奉斎している<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://ntomishrine.wixsite.com/official |title=登彌神社 |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref>。
== 祭神 == 本殿が、南向きで西本殿と東本殿が横並びとなっており、西本殿の祭神が[[神皇産霊神]]・饒速日命・[[天児屋命]]、東本殿の祭神が[[高皇産霊神]]・[[誉田別命]]。
== 文化財 == *文化庁より、令和２年４月３日、本殿・拝殿・神饌所・手水舎・社務所が登録有形文化財に登録される。 *[[粥占い]]が、昭和57年３月１日、奈良市無形民俗文化財に指定される。 == 境内神社 == *祓殿神社(祭神：[[瀬織津比売|瀬織津比売神]]・[[速秋津比売神]]・[[気吹戸主神]]・[[速佐須良比売神]]・[[表筒男神]]・[[中筒男神]]・[[底筒男神]]) *山室神社(祭神:[[大物主神]]・[[菅原道真公]]) *豊穂神社(祭神：[[大日孁|大日孁命]]・[[豊受比売|豊受比売神]]・天宇受女神) *荒神神社(祭神:[[大山祇神]]・庭高津日神) *比良田神社(祭神:[[猿田彦神]]・[[大己貴神]]・[[八重事代主神]])
== アクセス == *[[近鉄橿原線]]、[[九条駅 (奈良県)|九条駅]]より西へ２km(徒歩20分)
*駐車場あり

{{混同|同県桜井市にある[[等彌神社]]}} {{神社 |名称 = 登彌神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=奈良市}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|34|39|38|N|135|45|57|E}}}} |所在地 = {{JPN}} [[奈良県]][[奈良市]]石木町648-1 |祭神 = [[神皇産霊神]]・[[櫛玉饒速日命]]・[[天児屋命]]・[[高皇産霊神]]・[[誉田別命]] |神体 = |社格 = [[式内社]](旧[[県社]]) |創建 = |本殿 = [[春日造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 十月八日、九日 |神事 = | ISO = JP-29 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 39 | 緯度秒 = 38 | 経度度 = 135 | 経度分 = 45 | 経度秒 = 57 | 地図 = Japan Nara }} '''登彌神社'''（とみじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[奈良市]]石木町に[[鎮座]]する[[式内社]]<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=延喜式神明帳 |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref>で、旧社格は、[[県社]]([[昭和]]14年に列格)。神社の北の明神山からは、祭祀[[土器]]が出土している。
由緒については、[[皇紀]]４年春２月23日、[[神武天皇]]がこの地に於いて[[皇祖神|皇祖天神]](または、[[饒速日命]])を祭祀されたのが、淵源であり、その後、登美連が祖先である饒速日命の居住地であったこの地に、命の夫妻を奉祀したのが神社の創建とされている。小宮も合わせて二十二柱の神を奉斎している<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://ntomishrine.wixsite.com/official |title=登彌神社 |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref>。
== 祭神 == 本殿が、南向きで西本殿と東本殿が横並びとなっており、西本殿の祭神が[[神皇産霊神]]・饒速日命・[[天児屋命]]、東本殿の祭神が[[高皇産霊神]]・[[誉田別命]]。なお、饒速日命は、登美饒速日命・木嶋大明神とも呼ばれている。
== 文化財 == *文化庁より、令和２年４月３日、本殿・拝殿・神饌所・手水舎・社務所が登録有形文化財に登録される。 *[[粥占い]]が、昭和57年３月１日、奈良市無形民俗文化財に指定される。 == 境内神社 == *祓殿神社(祭神：[[瀬織津比売|瀬織津比売神]]・[[速秋津比売神]]・[[気吹戸主神]]・[[速佐須良比売神]]・[[表筒男神]]・[[中筒男神]]・[[底筒男神]]) *山室神社(祭神:[[大物主神]]・[[菅原道真公]]) *豊穂神社(祭神：[[大日孁|大日孁命]]・[[豊受比売|豊受比売神]]・天宇受女神) *荒神神社(祭神:[[大山祇神]]・庭高津日神) *比良田神社(祭神:[[猿田彦神]]・[[大己貴神]]・[[八重事代主神]])
== アクセス == *[[近鉄橿原線]]、[[九条駅 (奈良県)|九条駅]]より西へ２km(徒歩20分)
*駐車場あり
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}

{{nihongo|'''Tomi Shrine'''|登彌神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

== P31 (P31) ==

{{nihongo|'''Tomi Shrine'''|登彌神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tomi Shrine'''|登彌神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tomi Shrine'''|登彌神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tomi Shrine'''|登彌神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tomi Shrine'''|登彌神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tomi Shrine'''|登彌神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tomi Shrine'''|登彌神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tomi Shrine'''|登彌神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tomi Shrine'''|登彌神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tomi Shrine'''|登彌神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tomi Shrine'''|登彌神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tomi Shrine'''|登彌神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tomi Shrine'''|登彌神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tomi Shrine'''|登彌神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

{{混同|同県桜井市にある[[等彌神社]]}} {{神社 |名称 = 登彌神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=奈良市}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|34|39|38|N|135|45|57|E}}}} |所在地 = {{JPN}} [[奈良県]][[奈良市]]石木町648-1 |祭神 = [[神皇産霊神]]・[[櫛玉饒速日命]]・[[天児屋命]]・[[高皇産霊神]]・[[誉田別命]] |神体 = |社格 = [[式内社]]、旧[[県社]] |創建 = |本殿 = [[春日造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 十月八日、九日 |神事 = | ISO = JP-29 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 39 | 緯度秒 = 38 | 経度度 = 135 | 経度分 = 45 | 経度秒 = 57 | 地図 = Japan Nara }} '''登彌神社'''（とみじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[奈良市]]石木町に[[鎮座]]する[[式内社]]<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=延喜式神明帳 |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref>で、旧社格は、[[県社]]([[昭和]]14年に列格)。神社の北の明神山からは、祭祀[[土器]]が出土している。
由緒については、[[皇紀]]４年春２月23日、[[神武天皇]]がこの地に於いて[[皇祖神|皇祖天神]](または、[[饒速日命]])を祭祀されたのが、淵源であり、その後、登美連が祖先である饒速日命の居住地であったこの地に、命の夫妻を奉祀したのが神社の創建とされている。小宮も合わせて二十二柱の神を奉斎している<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://ntomishrine.wixsite.com/official |title=登彌神社 |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref>。
== 祭神 == 本殿が、南向きで西本殿と東本殿が横並びとなっており、西本殿の祭神が[[神皇産霊神]]・饒速日命・[[天児屋命]]、東本殿の祭神が[[高皇産霊神]]・[[誉田別命]]。なお、饒速日命は、登美饒速日命・木嶋大明神とも呼ばれている。
== 文化財 == *文化庁より、令和２年４月３日、本殿・拝殿・神饌所・手水舎・社務所が登録有形文化財に登録される。 *[[粥占い]]が、昭和57年３月１日、奈良市無形民俗文化財に指定される。 == 境内神社 == *祓殿神社(祭神：[[瀬織津比売|瀬織津比売神]]・[[速秋津比売神]]・[[気吹戸主神]]・[[速佐須良比売神]]・[[表筒男神]]・[[中筒男神]]・[[底筒男神]]) *山室神社(祭神:[[大物主神]]・[[菅原道真公]]) *豊穂神社(祭神：[[大日孁|大日孁命]]・[[豊受比売|豊受比売神]]・天宇受女神) *荒神神社(祭神:[[大山祇神]]・庭高津日神) *比良田神社(祭神:[[猿田彦神]]・[[大己貴神]]・[[八重事代主神]])
== アクセス == *[[近鉄橿原線]]、[[九条駅 (奈良県)|九条駅]]より西へ２km(徒歩20分)
*駐車場あり
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
<!-- == 外部リンク == --> {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とみしんしや}} [[Category:奈良市の神社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧県社]] [[Category:奈良市の建築物]] [[Category:奈良市の歴史]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]]

{{Infobox religious building | name = Tomi Shrine | native_name = 登彌神社 | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Tomi Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.661059|135.765889}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Kamimusubi|lt=Kamimusubi|en|Kamimusubi|es|Kami-musubi-no-kami|fr|Kamimusubi|id|Kamimusubi|ja|カムムスヒ|mni|ꯀꯃꯤꯃꯨꯁꯨꯕꯤ|pl|Kamimusubi|ru|Камимусуби-но ками|sk|Kamimusubi|sv|Kami-Musubi|zh|神皇產靈尊|WD=Q3275901}} }}
{{nihongo|'''Tomi Shrine'''|登彌神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].
== instance of (P31) ==

{{translated page|ja|登彌神社|version=106927064|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]]
== Japanese Wikipedia content == {{混同|同県桜井市にある[[等彌神社]]}} {{神社 |名称 = 登彌神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=奈良市}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|34|39|38|N|135|45|57|E}}}} |所在地 = {{JPN}} [[奈良県]][[奈良市]]石木町648-1 |祭神 = [[神皇産霊神]]・[[櫛玉饒速日命]]・[[天児屋命]]・[[高皇産霊神]]・[[誉田別命]] |神体 = |社格 = [[式内社]]、旧[[県社]] |創建 = |本殿 = [[春日造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 十月八日、九日 |神事 = | ISO = JP-29 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 39 | 緯度秒 = 38 | 経度度 = 135 | 経度分 = 45 | 経度秒 = 57 | 地図 = Japan Nara }} '''登彌神社'''（とみじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[奈良市]]石木町に[[鎮座]]する[[式内社]]<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=延喜式神明帳 |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref>で、旧社格は、[[県社]]([[昭和]]14年に列格)。神社の北の明神山からは、祭祀[[土器]]が出土している。
由緒については、[[皇紀]]４年春２月23日、[[神武天皇]]がこの地に於いて[[皇祖神|皇祖天神]](または、[[饒速日命]])を祭祀されたのが、淵源であり、その後、登美連が祖先である饒速日命の居住地であったこの地に、命の夫妻を奉祀したのが神社の創建とされている。小宮も合わせて二十二柱の神を奉斎している<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://ntomishrine.wixsite.com/official |title=登彌神社 |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref>。
== 祭神 == 本殿が、南向きで西本殿と東本殿が横並びとなっており、西本殿の祭神が[[神皇産霊神]]・饒速日命・[[天児屋命]]、東本殿の祭神が[[高皇産霊神]]・[[誉田別命]]。なお、饒速日命は、登美饒速日命・木嶋大明神とも呼ばれている。
== 文化財 == *文化庁より、令和２年４月３日、本殿・拝殿・神饌所・手水舎・社務所が登録有形文化財に登録される。 *[[粥占い]]が、昭和57年３月１日、奈良市無形民俗文化財に指定される。 == 境内神社 == *祓殿神社(祭神：[[瀬織津比売|瀬織津比売神]]・[[速秋津比売神]]・[[気吹戸主神]]・[[速佐須良比売神]]・[[表筒男神]]・[[中筒男神]]・[[底筒男神]]) *山室神社(祭神:[[大物主神]]・[[菅原道真公]]) *豊穂神社(祭神：[[大日孁|大日孁命]]・[[豊受比売|豊受比売神]]・天宇受女神) *荒神神社(祭神:[[大山祇神]]・庭高津日神) *比良田神社(祭神:[[猿田彦神]]・[[大己貴神]]・[[八重事代主神]])
== アクセス == *[[近鉄橿原線]]、[[九条駅 (奈良県)|九条駅]]より西へ２km(徒歩20分)
*駐車場あり
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}

{{nihongo|'''Tomi Shrine'''|登彌神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

{{translated page|ja|登彌神社|version=106927064|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]]
== Japanese Wikipedia content == {{混同|同県桜井市にある[[等彌神社]]}} {{神社 |名称 = 登彌神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=奈良市}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|34|39|38|N|135|45|57|E}}}} |所在地 = {{JPN}} [[奈良県]][[奈良市]]石木町648-1 |祭神 = [[神皇産霊神]]・[[櫛玉饒速日命]]・[[天児屋命]]・[[高皇産霊神]]・[[誉田別命]] |神体 = |社格 = [[式内社]]、旧[[県社]] |創建 = |本殿 = [[春日造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 十月八日、九日 |神事 = | ISO = JP-29 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 39 | 緯度秒 = 38 | 経度度 = 135 | 経度分 = 45 | 経度秒 = 57 | 地図 = Japan Nara }} '''登彌神社'''（とみじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[奈良市]]石木町に[[鎮座]]する[[式内社]]<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=延喜式神明帳 |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref>で、旧社格は、[[県社]]([[昭和]]14年に列格)。神社の北の明神山からは、祭祀[[土器]]が出土している。
由緒については、[[皇紀]]４年春２月23日、[[神武天皇]]がこの地に於いて[[皇祖神|皇祖天神]](または、[[饒速日命]])を祭祀されたのが、淵源であり、その後、登美連が祖先である饒速日命の居住地であったこの地に、命の夫妻を奉祀したのが神社の創建とされている。小宮も合わせて二十二柱の神を奉斎している<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://ntomishrine.wixsite.com/official |title=登彌神社 |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref>。
== 祭神 == 本殿が、南向きで西本殿と東本殿が横並びとなっており、西本殿の祭神が[[神皇産霊神]]・饒速日命・[[天児屋命]]、東本殿の祭神が[[高皇産霊神]]・[[誉田別命]]。なお、饒速日命は、登美饒速日命・木嶋大明神とも呼ばれている。
== 文化財 == *文化庁より、令和２年４月３日、本殿・拝殿・神饌所・手水舎・社務所が登録有形文化財に登録される。 *[[粥占い]]が、昭和57年３月１日、奈良市無形民俗文化財に指定される。 == 境内神社 == *祓殿神社(祭神：[[瀬織津比売|瀬織津比売神]]・[[速秋津比売神]]・[[気吹戸主神]]・[[速佐須良比売神]]・[[表筒男神]]・[[中筒男神]]・[[底筒男神]]) *山室神社(祭神:[[大物主神]]・[[菅原道真公]]) *豊穂神社(祭神：[[大日孁|大日孁命]]・[[豊受比売|豊受比売神]]・天宇受女神) *荒神神社(祭神:[[大山祇神]]・庭高津日神) *比良田神社(祭神:[[猿田彦神]]・[[大己貴神]]・[[八重事代主神]])
== アクセス == *[[近鉄橿原線]]、[[九条駅 (奈良県)|九条駅]]より西へ２km(徒歩20分)
*駐車場あり
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}

{{nihongo|'''Tomi Shrine'''|登彌神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

== Japanese Wikipedia content == {{混同|同県桜井市にある[[等彌神社]]}} {{神社 |名称 = 登彌神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=奈良市}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|34|39|38|N|135|45|57|E}}}} |所在地 = {{JPN}} [[奈良県]][[奈良市]]石木町648-1 |祭神 = [[神皇産霊神]]・[[櫛玉饒速日命]]・[[天児屋命]]・[[高皇産霊神]]・[[誉田別命]] |神体 = |社格 = [[式内社]]、旧[[県社]] |創建 = |本殿 = [[春日造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 十月八日、九日 |神事 = | ISO = JP-29 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 39 | 緯度秒 = 38 | 経度度 = 135 | 経度分 = 45 | 経度秒 = 57 | 地図 = Japan Nara }} '''登彌神社'''（とみじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[奈良市]]石木町に[[鎮座]]する[[式内社]]<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=延喜式神明帳 |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref>で、旧社格は、[[県社]]([[昭和]]14年に列格)。神社の北の明神山からは、祭祀[[土器]]が出土している。
由緒については、[[皇紀]]４年春２月23日、[[神武天皇]]がこの地に於いて[[皇祖神|皇祖天神]](または、[[饒速日命]])を祭祀されたのが、淵源であり、その後、登美連が祖先である饒速日命の居住地であったこの地に、命の夫妻を奉祀したのが神社の創建とされている。小宮も合わせて二十二柱の神を奉斎している<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://ntomishrine.wixsite.com/official |title=登彌神社 |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref>。
== 祭神 == 本殿が、南向きで西本殿と東本殿が横並びとなっており、西本殿の祭神が[[神皇産霊神]]・饒速日命・[[天児屋命]]、東本殿の祭神が[[高皇産霊神]]・[[誉田別命]]。なお、饒速日命は、登美饒速日命・木嶋大明神とも呼ばれている。
== 文化財 == *文化庁より、令和２年４月３日、本殿・拝殿・神饌所・手水舎・社務所が登録有形文化財に登録される。 *[[粥占い]]が、昭和57年３月１日、奈良市無形民俗文化財に指定される。 == 境内神社 == *祓殿神社(祭神：[[瀬織津比売|瀬織津比売神]]・[[速秋津比売神]]・[[気吹戸主神]]・[[速佐須良比売神]]・[[表筒男神]]・[[中筒男神]]・[[底筒男神]]) *山室神社(祭神:[[大物主神]]・[[菅原道真公]]) *豊穂神社(祭神：[[大日孁|大日孁命]]・[[豊受比売|豊受比売神]]・天宇受女神) *荒神神社(祭神:[[大山祇神]]・庭高津日神) *比良田神社(祭神:[[猿田彦神]]・[[大己貴神]]・[[八重事代主神]])
== アクセス == *[[近鉄橿原線]]、[[九条駅 (奈良県)|九条駅]]より西へ２km(徒歩20分)
*駐車場あり
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
<!-- == 外部リンク == --> {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}}
==End Japanese== [[Category:奈良市の神社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧県社]] [[Category:奈良市の建築物]] [[Category:奈良市の歴史]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]]

== Japanese Wikipedia content == {{混同|同県桜井市にある{{jalink|等彌神社|Q11602724}}}} {{神社 |名称 = 登彌神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=奈良市}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|34|39|38|N|135|45|57|E}}}} |所在地 = {{JPN}} {{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|奈良市|Q169134}}石木町648-1 |祭神 = {{jalink|神皇産霊神}}・{{jalink|櫛玉饒速日命}}・{{jalink|天児屋命|Q2564508}}・{{jalink|高皇産霊神}}・{{jalink|誉田別命}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}、旧{{jalink|県社}} |創建 = |本殿 = {{jalink|春日造|Q3193781}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 十月八日、九日 |神事 = | ISO = JP-29 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 39 | 緯度秒 = 38 | 経度度 = 135 | 経度分 = 45 | 経度秒 = 57 | 地図 = Japan Nara }} '''登彌神社'''（とみじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|奈良市|Q169134}}石木町に{{jalink|鎮座}}する{{jalink|式内社}}<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=延喜式神明帳 |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref>で、旧社格は、{{jalink|県社}}({{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}14年に列格)。神社の北の明神山からは、祭祀{{jalink|土器|Q1377111}}が出土している。
由緒については、{{jalink|皇紀}}４年春２月23日、{{jalink|神武天皇|Q200188}}がこの地に於いて{{jalink|皇祖神|Q11581090|皇祖天神}}(または、{{jalink|饒速日命}})を祭祀されたのが、淵源であり、その後、登美連が祖先である饒速日命の居住地であったこの地に、命の夫妻を奉祀したのが神社の創建とされている。小宮も合わせて二十二柱の神を奉斎している<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://ntomishrine.wixsite.com/official |title=登彌神社 |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref>。
== 祭神 == 本殿が、南向きで西本殿と東本殿が横並びとなっており、西本殿の祭神が{{jalink|神皇産霊神}}・饒速日命・{{jalink|天児屋命|Q2564508}}、東本殿の祭神が{{jalink|高皇産霊神}}・{{jalink|誉田別命}}。なお、饒速日命は、登美饒速日命・木嶋大明神とも呼ばれている。
== 文化財 == *文化庁より、令和２年４月３日、本殿・拝殿・神饌所・手水舎・社務所が登録有形文化財に登録される。 *{{jalink|粥占い}}が、昭和57年３月１日、奈良市無形民俗文化財に指定される。 == 境内神社 == *祓殿神社(祭神：{{jalink|瀬織津比売||瀬織津比売神}}・{{jalink|速秋津比売神}}・{{jalink|気吹戸主神|Q86734190}}・{{jalink|速佐須良比売神|Q136731213}}・{{jalink|表筒男神}}・{{jalink|中筒男神}}・{{jalink|底筒男神}}) *山室神社(祭神:{{jalink|大物主神}}・{{jalink|菅原道真公}}) *豊穂神社(祭神：{{jalink|大日孁||大日孁命}}・{{jalink|豊受比売||豊受比売神}}・天宇受女神) *荒神神社(祭神:{{jalink|大山祇神}}・庭高津日神) *比良田神社(祭神:{{jalink|猿田彦神}}・{{jalink|大己貴神}}・{{jalink|八重事代主神}})
== アクセス == *{{jalink|近鉄橿原線|Q2491066}}、{{jalink|九条駅 (奈良県)|Q6442373|九条駅}}より西へ２km(徒歩20分)
*駐車場あり
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}

{{nihongo|'''Tomi Shrine'''|登彌神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tomi Shrine'''|登彌神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

== Japanese content ==

'''登彌神社'''（とみじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|奈良市|Q169134}}石木町に{{jalink|鎮座}}する{{jalink|式内社}}<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=延喜式神明帳 |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref>で、旧社格は、{{jalink|県社}}({{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}14年に列格)。神社の北の明神山からは、祭祀{{jalink|土器|Q1377111}}が出土している。

由緒については、{{jalink|皇紀}}４年春２月23日、{{jalink|神武天皇|Q200188}}がこの地に於いて{{jalink|皇祖神|Q11581090|皇祖天神}}(または、{{jalink|饒速日命}})を祭祀されたのが、淵源であり、その後、登美連が祖先である饒速日命の居住地であったこの地に、命の夫妻を奉祀したのが神社の創建とされている。小宮も合わせて二十二柱の神を奉斎している<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://ntomishrine.wixsite.com/official |title=登彌神社 |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref>。

=== 祭神 ===
本殿が、南向きで西本殿と東本殿が横並びとなっており、西本殿の祭神が{{jalink|神皇産霊神}}・饒速日命・{{jalink|天児屋命|Q2564508}}、東本殿の祭神が{{jalink|高皇産霊神}}・{{jalink|誉田別命}}。なお、饒速日命は、登美饒速日命・木嶋大明神とも呼ばれている。

=== 文化財 ===
*文化庁より、令和２年４月３日、本殿・拝殿・神饌所・手水舎・社務所が登録有形文化財に登録される。
*{{jalink|粥占い}}が、昭和57年３月１日、奈良市無形民俗文化財に指定される。
=== 境内神社 ===
*祓殿神社(祭神：{{jalink|瀬織津比売||瀬織津比売神}}・{{jalink|速秋津比売神}}・{{jalink|気吹戸主神|Q86734190}}・{{jalink|速佐須良比売神|Q136731213}}・{{jalink|表筒男神}}・{{jalink|中筒男神}}・{{jalink|底筒男神}})
*山室神社(祭神:{{jalink|大物主神}}・{{jalink|菅原道真公}})
*豊穂神社(祭神：{{jalink|大日孁||大日孁命}}・{{jalink|豊受比売||豊受比売神}}・天宇受女神)
*荒神神社(祭神:{{jalink|大山祇神}}・庭高津日神)
*比良田神社(祭神:{{jalink|猿田彦神}}・{{jalink|大己貴神}}・{{jalink|八重事代主神}})

=== アクセス ===
*{{jalink|近鉄橿原線|Q2491066}}、{{jalink|九条駅 (奈良県)|Q6442373|九条駅}}より西へ２km(徒歩20分)

*駐車場あり

{{nihongo|'''Tomi Shrine'''|登彌神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

== Japanese content ==
{{混同|同県桜井市にある{{jalink|等彌神社|Q11602724}}}}

'''登彌神社'''（とみじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|奈良市|Q169134}}石木町に{{jalink|鎮座}}する{{jalink|式内社}}<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=延喜式神明帳 |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref>で、旧社格は、{{jalink|県社}}({{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}14年に列格)。神社の北の明神山からは、祭祀{{jalink|土器|Q1377111}}が出土している。

由緒については、{{jalink|皇紀}}４年春２月23日、{{jalink|神武天皇|Q200188}}がこの地に於いて{{jalink|皇祖神|Q11581090|皇祖天神}}(または、{{jalink|饒速日命}})を祭祀されたのが、淵源であり、その後、登美連が祖先である饒速日命の居住地であったこの地に、命の夫妻を奉祀したのが神社の創建とされている。小宮も合わせて二十二柱の神を奉斎している<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://ntomishrine.wixsite.com/official |title=登彌神社 |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref>。

=== 祭神 ===
本殿が、南向きで西本殿と東本殿が横並びとなっており、西本殿の祭神が{{jalink|神皇産霊神}}・饒速日命・{{jalink|天児屋命|Q2564508}}、東本殿の祭神が{{jalink|高皇産霊神}}・{{jalink|誉田別命}}。なお、饒速日命は、登美饒速日命・木嶋大明神とも呼ばれている。

=== 文化財 ===
*文化庁より、令和２年４月３日、本殿・拝殿・神饌所・手水舎・社務所が登録有形文化財に登録される。
*{{jalink|粥占い}}が、昭和57年３月１日、奈良市無形民俗文化財に指定される。
=== 境内神社 ===
*祓殿神社(祭神：{{jalink|瀬織津比売||瀬織津比売神}}・{{jalink|速秋津比売神}}・{{jalink|気吹戸主神|Q86734190}}・{{jalink|速佐須良比売神|Q136731213}}・{{jalink|表筒男神}}・{{jalink|中筒男神}}・{{jalink|底筒男神}})
*山室神社(祭神:{{jalink|大物主神}}・{{jalink|菅原道真公}})
*豊穂神社(祭神：{{jalink|大日孁||大日孁命}}・{{jalink|豊受比売||豊受比売神}}・天宇受女神)
*荒神神社(祭神:{{jalink|大山祇神}}・庭高津日神)
*比良田神社(祭神:{{jalink|猿田彦神}}・{{jalink|大己貴神}}・{{jalink|八重事代主神}})

=== アクセス ===
*{{jalink|近鉄橿原線|Q2491066}}、{{jalink|九条駅 (奈良県)|Q6442373|九条駅}}より西へ２km(徒歩20分)

*駐車場あり

{{nihongo|'''Tomi Shrine'''|登彌神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

}}
'''登彌神社'''（とみじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|奈良市|Q169134}}石木町に{{jalink|鎮座}}する{{jalink|式内社}}<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=延喜式神明帳 |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref>で、旧社格は、{{jalink|県社}}({{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}14年に列格)。神社の北の明神山からは、祭祀{{jalink|土器|Q1377111}}が出土している。

由緒については、{{jalink|皇紀}}４年春２月23日、{{jalink|神武天皇|Q200188}}がこの地に於いて{{jalink|皇祖神|Q11581090|皇祖天神}}(または、{{jalink|饒速日命}})を祭祀されたのが、淵源であり、その後、登美連が祖先である饒速日命の居住地であったこの地に、命の夫妻を奉祀したのが神社の創建とされている。小宮も合わせて二十二柱の神を奉斎している<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://ntomishrine.wixsite.com/official |title=登彌神社 |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref>。

=== 祭神 ===
本殿が、南向きで西本殿と東本殿が横並びとなっており、西本殿の祭神が{{jalink|神皇産霊神}}・饒速日命・{{jalink|天児屋命|Q2564508}}、東本殿の祭神が{{jalink|高皇産霊神}}・{{jalink|誉田別命}}。なお、饒速日命は、登美饒速日命・木嶋大明神とも呼ばれている。

=== 文化財 ===
*文化庁より、令和２年４月３日、本殿・拝殿・神饌所・手水舎・社務所が登録有形文化財に登録される。
*{{jalink|粥占い}}が、昭和57年３月１日、奈良市無形民俗文化財に指定される。
=== 境内神社 ===
*祓殿神社(祭神：{{jalink|瀬織津比売||瀬織津比売神}}・{{jalink|速秋津比売神}}・{{jalink|気吹戸主神|Q86734190}}・{{jalink|速佐須良比売神|Q136731213}}・{{jalink|表筒男神}}・{{jalink|中筒男神}}・{{jalink|底筒男神}})
*山室神社(祭神:{{jalink|大物主神}}・{{jalink|菅原道真公}})
*豊穂神社(祭神：{{jalink|大日孁||大日孁命}}・{{jalink|豊受比売||豊受比売神}}・天宇受女神)
*荒神神社(祭神:{{jalink|大山祇神}}・庭高津日神)
*比良田神社(祭神:{{jalink|猿田彦神}}・{{jalink|大己貴神}}・{{jalink|八重事代主神}})

=== アクセス ===
*{{jalink|近鉄橿原線|Q2491066}}、{{jalink|九条駅 (奈良県)|Q6442373|九条駅}}より西へ２km(徒歩20分)

*駐車場あり

{{nihongo|'''Tomi Shrine'''|登彌神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

'''登彌神社'''（とみじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|奈良市|Q169134}}石木町に{{jalink|鎮座}}する{{jalink|式内社}}<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=延喜式神明帳 |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref>で、旧社格は、{{jalink|県社}}({{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}14年に列格)。神社の北の明神山からは、祭祀{{jalink|土器|Q1377111}}が出土している。

由緒については、{{jalink|皇紀}}４年春２月23日、{{jalink|神武天皇|Q200188}}がこの地に於いて{{jalink|皇祖神|Q11581090|皇祖天神}}(または、{{jalink|饒速日命}})を祭祀されたのが、淵源であり、その後、登美連が祖先である饒速日命の居住地であったこの地に、命の夫妻を奉祀したのが神社の創建とされている。小宮も合わせて二十二柱の神を奉斎している<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://ntomishrine.wixsite.com/official |title=登彌神社 |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref>。

=== 祭神 ===
本殿が、南向きで西本殿と東本殿が横並びとなっており、西本殿の祭神が{{jalink|神皇産霊神}}・饒速日命・{{jalink|天児屋命|Q2564508}}、東本殿の祭神が{{jalink|高皇産霊神}}・{{jalink|誉田別命}}。なお、饒速日命は、登美饒速日命・木嶋大明神とも呼ばれている。

=== 文化財 ===
*文化庁より、令和２年４月３日、本殿・拝殿・神饌所・手水舎・社務所が登録有形文化財に登録される。
*{{jalink|粥占い}}が、昭和57年３月１日、奈良市無形民俗文化財に指定される。
=== 境内神社 ===
*祓殿神社(祭神：{{jalink|瀬織津比売||瀬織津比売神}}・{{jalink|速秋津比売神}}・{{jalink|気吹戸主神|Q86734190}}・{{jalink|速佐須良比売神|Q136731213}}・{{jalink|表筒男神}}・{{jalink|中筒男神}}・{{jalink|底筒男神}})
*山室神社(祭神:{{jalink|大物主神}}・{{jalink|菅原道真公}})
*豊穂神社(祭神：{{jalink|大日孁||大日孁命}}・{{jalink|豊受比売||豊受比売神}}・天宇受女神)
*荒神神社(祭神:{{jalink|大山祇神}}・庭高津日神)
*比良田神社(祭神:{{jalink|猿田彦神}}・{{jalink|大己貴神}}・{{jalink|八重事代主神}})

=== アクセス ===
*{{jalink|近鉄橿原線|Q2491066}}、{{jalink|九条駅 (奈良県)|Q6442373|九条駅}}より西へ２km(徒歩20分)

*駐車場あり

'''登彌神社'''（とみじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|奈良市|Q169134}}石木町に{{jalink|鎮座}}する{{jalink|式内社}}<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=延喜式神明帳 |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref>で、旧社格は、{{jalink|県社}}({{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}14年に列格)。神社の北の明神山からは、祭祀{{jalink|土器|Q1377111}}が出土している。

由緒については、{{jalink|皇紀}}４年春２月23日、{{jalink|神武天皇|Q200188}}がこの地に於いて{{jalink|皇祖神|Q11581090|皇祖天神}}(または、{{jalink|饒速日命}})を祭祀されたのが、淵源であり、その後、登美連が祖先である饒速日命の居住地であったこの地に、命の夫妻を奉祀したのが神社の創建とされている。小宮も合わせて二十二柱の神を奉斎している<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://ntomishrine.wixsite.com/official |title=登彌神社 |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref>。

=== 祭神 ===
本殿が、南向きで西本殿と東本殿が横並びとなっており、西本殿の祭神が{{jalink|神皇産霊神}}・饒速日命・{{jalink|天児屋命|Q2564508}}、東本殿の祭神が{{jalink|高皇産霊神}}・{{jalink|誉田別命}}。なお、饒速日命は、登美饒速日命・木嶋大明神とも呼ばれている。

=== 文化財 ===
*文化庁より、令和２年４月３日、本殿・拝殿・神饌所・手水舎・社務所が登録有形文化財に登録される。
*{{jalink|粥占い}}が、昭和57年３月１日、奈良市無形民俗文化財に指定される。
=== 境内神社 ===
*祓殿神社(祭神：{{jalink|瀬織津比売||瀬織津比売神}}・{{jalink|速秋津比売神}}・{{jalink|気吹戸主神|Q86734190}}・{{jalink|速佐須良比売神|Q136731213}}・{{jalink|表筒男神}}・{{jalink|中筒男神}}・{{jalink|底筒男神}})
*山室神社(祭神:{{jalink|大物主神}}・{{jalink|菅原道真公}})
*豊穂神社(祭神：{{jalink|大日孁||大日孁命}}・{{jalink|豊受比売||豊受比売神}}・天宇受女神)
*荒神神社(祭神:{{jalink|大山祇神}}・庭高津日神)
*比良田神社(祭神:{{jalink|猿田彦神}}・{{jalink|大己貴神}}・{{jalink|八重事代主神}})

=== アクセス ===
*{{jalink|近鉄橿原線|Q2491066}}、{{jalink|九条駅 (奈良県)|Q6442373|九条駅}}より西へ２km(徒歩20分)

*駐車場あり

== Japanese content ==
{{混同|同県桜井市にある{{jalink|等彌神社|Q11602724}}}}

'''登彌神社'''（とみじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|奈良市|Q169134}}石木町に{{jalink|鎮座}}する{{jalink|式内社}}<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=延喜式神明帳 |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref>で、旧社格は、{{jalink|県社}}({{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}14年に列格)。神社の北の明神山からは、祭祀{{jalink|土器|Q1377111}}が出土している。

由緒については、{{jalink|皇紀}}４年春２月23日、{{jalink|神武天皇|Q200188}}がこの地に於いて{{jalink|皇祖神|Q11581090|皇祖天神}}(または、{{jalink|饒速日命}})を祭祀されたのが、淵源であり、その後、登美連が祖先である饒速日命の居住地であったこの地に、命の夫妻を奉祀したのが神社の創建とされている。小宮も合わせて二十二柱の神を奉斎している<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://ntomishrine.wixsite.com/official |title=登彌神社 |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref>。

=== 祭神 ===
本殿が、南向きで西本殿と東本殿が横並びとなっており、西本殿の祭神が{{jalink|神皇産霊神}}・饒速日命・{{jalink|天児屋命|Q2564508}}、東本殿の祭神が{{jalink|高皇産霊神}}・{{jalink|誉田別命}}。なお、饒速日命は、登美饒速日命・木嶋大明神とも呼ばれている。

=== 文化財 ===
*文化庁より、令和２年４月３日、本殿・拝殿・神饌所・手水舎・社務所が登録有形文化財に登録される。
*{{jalink|粥占い}}が、昭和57年３月１日、奈良市無形民俗文化財に指定される。
=== 境内神社 ===
*祓殿神社(祭神：{{jalink|瀬織津比売||瀬織津比売神}}・{{jalink|速秋津比売神}}・{{jalink|気吹戸主神|Q86734190}}・{{jalink|速佐須良比売神|Q136731213}}・{{jalink|表筒男神}}・{{jalink|中筒男神}}・{{jalink|底筒男神}})
*山室神社(祭神:{{jalink|大物主神}}・{{jalink|菅原道真公}})
*豊穂神社(祭神：{{jalink|大日孁||大日孁命}}・{{jalink|豊受比売||豊受比売神}}・天宇受女神)
*荒神神社(祭神:{{jalink|大山祇神}}・庭高津日神)
*比良田神社(祭神:{{jalink|猿田彦神}}・{{jalink|大己貴神}}・{{jalink|八重事代主神}})

=== アクセス ===
*{{jalink|近鉄橿原線|Q2491066}}、{{jalink|九条駅 (奈良県)|Q6442373|九条駅}}より西へ２km(徒歩20分)

*駐車場あり

{{nihongo|'''Tomi Shrine'''|登彌神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

== instance of (P31) ==

== Japanese content ==

'''Tomi Shrine''' (とみじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=Engishiki Jinmeichō |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref> {{jalink|enshrined|Q927365}} in Ishiki-chō, {{jalink|Nara|Q169134}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. Its former shrine rank was {{jalink|prefectural shrine}} (ranked in {{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 14). Ritual {{jalink|earthenware|Q1377111}} has been excavated from Myōjin-yama north of the shrine.

According to tradition, the origin is that on Kōki 4 (spring, February 23), {{jalink|Emperor Jinmu|Q200188}} worshiped the {{jalink|Imperial ancestral deities|Q11581090|imperial heavenly ancestors}} (or {{jalink|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto}}) at this place. Later, the Tomi-no-Muraji clan enshrined Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and his consort in this area where their ancestor had resided. Including smaller shrines, 22 deities are worshiped<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://ntomishrine.wixsite.com/official |title=Tomi Shrine |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref>.

=== Enshrined deities ===
The honden faces south, with western and eastern honden side by side. Deities of the western honden are {{jalink|Kamimusubi-no-Kami}}, Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto, and {{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508}}; deities of the eastern honden are {{jalink|Takamimusubi-no-Kami}} and {{jalink|Honda-wake-no-Mikoto}}. Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto is also called Tomi Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and Kijima Daimyōjin.

=== Cultural properties ===
*On April 3, Reiwa 2, the honden, haiden, shinsenjo, chōzuya, and shrine office were registered as national tangible cultural properties by the Agency for Cultural Affairs.
*{{jalink|Kayu uranai}} was designated an intangible folk cultural property of Nara City on March 1, Shōwa 57.
=== Precinct shrines ===
*Haraedo Shrine (deities: {{jalink|Seoritsuhime-no-Kami||Seoritsuhime-no-Kami}}, {{jalink|Hayakitsuhime-no-Kami}}, {{jalink|Ibukido-nushi-no-Kami|Q86734190}}, {{jalink|Hayasasurahime-no-Kami|Q136731213}}, {{jalink|Uwatsutsuno-o-no-Kami}}, {{jalink|Nakatsutsuno-o-no-Kami}}, {{jalink|Sokotsutsuno-o-no-Kami}})
*Yamamuro Shrine (deities: {{jalink|Ōmononushi-no-Kami}}, {{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane-kō}})
*Toyoho Shrine (deities: {{jalink|Ōhirume-no-Mikoto||Ōhirume-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Toyoukehime-no-Kami||Toyoukehime-no-Kami}}, Ame-no-Uzume-no-Kami)
*Kōjin Shrine (deities: {{jalink|Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami}}, Niwatakatsuhi-no-Kami)
*Hirata Shrine (deities: {{jalink|Sarutahiko-no-Kami}}, {{jalink|Ōnamuchi-no-Kami}}, {{jalink|Yae-Kotoshironushi-no-Kami}})

=== Access ===
*2 km west from {{jalink|Kujō Station (Nara)|Q6442373|Kujō Station}} on the {{jalink|Kintetsu Kashihara Line|Q2491066}} (about 20 minutes on foot)

*Parking available

{{nihongo|'''Tomi Shrine'''|登彌神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

== instance of (P31) ==

'''Tomi Shrine''' (とみじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=Engishiki Jinmeichō |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref> {{jalink|enshrined|Q927365}} in Ishiki-chō, {{jalink|Nara|Q169134}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. Its former shrine rank was {{jalink|prefectural shrine}} (ranked in {{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 14). Ritual {{jalink|earthenware|Q1377111}} has been excavated from Myōjin-yama north of the shrine.

According to tradition, the origin is that on Kōki 4 (spring, February 23), {{jalink|Emperor Jinmu|Q200188}} worshiped the {{jalink|Imperial ancestral deities|Q11581090|imperial heavenly ancestors}} (or {{jalink|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto}}) at this place. Later, the Tomi-no-Muraji clan enshrined Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and his consort in this area where their ancestor had resided. Including smaller shrines, 22 deities are worshiped<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://ntomishrine.wixsite.com/official |title=Tomi Shrine |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref>.

=== Enshrined deities ===
The honden faces south, with western and eastern honden side by side. Deities of the western honden are {{jalink|Kamimusubi-no-Kami}}, Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto, and {{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508}}; deities of the eastern honden are {{jalink|Takamimusubi-no-Kami}} and {{jalink|Honda-wake-no-Mikoto}}. Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto is also called Tomi Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and Kijima Daimyōjin.

=== Cultural properties ===
*On April 3, Reiwa 2, the honden, haiden, shinsenjo, chōzuya, and shrine office were registered as national tangible cultural properties by the Agency for Cultural Affairs.
*{{jalink|Kayu uranai}} was designated an intangible folk cultural property of Nara City on March 1, Shōwa 57.
=== Precinct shrines ===
*Haraedo Shrine (deities: {{jalink|Seoritsuhime-no-Kami||Seoritsuhime-no-Kami}}, {{jalink|Hayakitsuhime-no-Kami}}, {{jalink|Ibukido-nushi-no-Kami|Q86734190}}, {{jalink|Hayasasurahime-no-Kami|Q136731213}}, {{jalink|Uwatsutsuno-o-no-Kami}}, {{jalink|Nakatsutsuno-o-no-Kami}}, {{jalink|Sokotsutsuno-o-no-Kami}})
*Yamamuro Shrine (deities: {{jalink|Ōmononushi-no-Kami}}, {{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane-kō}})
*Toyoho Shrine (deities: {{jalink|Ōhirume-no-Mikoto||Ōhirume-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Toyoukehime-no-Kami||Toyoukehime-no-Kami}}, Ame-no-Uzume-no-Kami)
*Kōjin Shrine (deities: {{jalink|Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami}}, Niwatakatsuhi-no-Kami)
*Hirata Shrine (deities: {{jalink|Sarutahiko-no-Kami}}, {{jalink|Ōnamuchi-no-Kami}}, {{jalink|Yae-Kotoshironushi-no-Kami}})

=== Access ===
*2 km west from {{jalink|Kujō Station (Nara)|Q6442373|Kujō Station}} on the {{jalink|Kintetsu Kashihara Line|Q2491066}} (about 20 minutes on foot)

*Parking available

'''Tomi Shrine''' (とみじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=Engishiki Jinmeichō |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref> {{jalink|enshrined|Q927365}} in Ishiki-chō, {{jalink|Nara|Q169134}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. Its former shrine rank was {{jalink|prefectural shrine}} (ranked in {{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 14). Ritual {{jalink|earthenware|Q1377111}} has been excavated from Myōjin-yama north of the shrine.

According to tradition, the origin is that on Kōki 4 (spring, February 23), {{jalink|Emperor Jinmu|Q200188}} worshiped the {{jalink|Imperial ancestral deities|Q11581090|imperial heavenly ancestors}} (or {{jalink|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto}}) at this place. Later, the Tomi-no-Muraji clan enshrined Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and his consort in this area where their ancestor had resided. Including smaller shrines, 22 deities are worshiped<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://ntomishrine.wixsite.com/official |title=Tomi Shrine |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref>.

=== Enshrined deities ===
The honden faces south, with western and eastern honden side by side. Deities of the western honden are {{jalink|Kamimusubi-no-Kami}}, Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto, and {{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508}}; deities of the eastern honden are {{jalink|Takamimusubi-no-Kami}} and {{jalink|Honda-wake-no-Mikoto}}. Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto is also called Tomi Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and Kijima Daimyōjin.

=== Cultural properties ===
*On April 3, Reiwa 2, the honden, haiden, shinsenjo, chōzuya, and shrine office were registered as national tangible cultural properties by the Agency for Cultural Affairs.
*{{jalink|Kayu uranai}} was designated an intangible folk cultural property of Nara City on March 1, Shōwa 57.
=== Precinct shrines ===
*Haraedo Shrine (deities: {{jalink|Seoritsuhime-no-Kami||Seoritsuhime-no-Kami}}, {{jalink|Hayakitsuhime-no-Kami}}, {{jalink|Ibukido-nushi-no-Kami|Q86734190}}, {{jalink|Hayasasurahime-no-Kami|Q136731213}}, {{jalink|Uwatsutsuno-o-no-Kami}}, {{jalink|Nakatsutsuno-o-no-Kami}}, {{jalink|Sokotsutsuno-o-no-Kami}})
*Yamamuro Shrine (deities: {{jalink|Ōmononushi-no-Kami}}, {{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane-kō}})
*Toyoho Shrine (deities: {{jalink|Ōhirume-no-Mikoto||Ōhirume-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Toyoukehime-no-Kami||Toyoukehime-no-Kami}}, Ame-no-Uzume-no-Kami)
*Kōjin Shrine (deities: {{jalink|Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami}}, Niwatakatsuhi-no-Kami)
*Hirata Shrine (deities: {{jalink|Sarutahiko-no-Kami}}, {{jalink|Ōnamuchi-no-Kami}}, {{jalink|Yae-Kotoshironushi-no-Kami}})

=== Access ===
*2 km west from {{jalink|Kujō Station (Nara)|Q6442373|Kujō Station}} on the {{jalink|Kintetsu Kashihara Line|Q2491066}} (about 20 minutes on foot)

*Parking available

'''Tomi Shrine''' (とみじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=Engishiki Jinmeichō |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref> {{jalink|enshrined|Q927365}} in Ishiki-chō, {{jalink|Nara|Q169134}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. Its former shrine rank was {{jalink|prefectural shrine}} (ranked in {{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 14). Ritual {{jalink|earthenware|Q1377111}} has been excavated from Myōjin-yama north of the shrine.

According to tradition, the origin is that on Kōki 4 (spring, February 23), {{jalink|Emperor Jinmu|Q200188}} worshiped the {{jalink|Imperial ancestral deities|Q11581090|imperial heavenly ancestors}} (or {{jalink|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto}}) at this place. Later, the Tomi-no-Muraji clan enshrined Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and his consort in this area where their ancestor had resided. Including smaller shrines, 22 deities are worshiped<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://ntomishrine.wixsite.com/official |title=Tomi Shrine |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref>.

=== Enshrined deities ===
The honden faces south, with western and eastern honden side by side. Deities of the western honden are {{jalink|Kamimusubi-no-Kami}}, Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto, and {{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508}}; deities of the eastern honden are {{jalink|Takamimusubi-no-Kami}} and {{jalink|Honda-wake-no-Mikoto}}. Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto is also called Tomi Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and Kijima Daimyōjin.

=== Cultural properties ===
*On April 3, Reiwa 2, the honden, haiden, shinsenjo, chōzuya, and shrine office were registered as national tangible cultural properties by the Agency for Cultural Affairs.
*{{jalink|Kayu uranai}} was designated an intangible folk cultural property of Nara City on March 1, Shōwa 57.
=== Precinct shrines ===
*Haraedo Shrine (deities: {{jalink|Seoritsuhime-no-Kami||Seoritsuhime-no-Kami}}, {{jalink|Hayakitsuhime-no-Kami}}, {{jalink|Ibukido-nushi-no-Kami|Q86734190}}, {{jalink|Hayasasurahime-no-Kami|Q136731213}}, {{jalink|Uwatsutsuno-o-no-Kami}}, {{jalink|Nakatsutsuno-o-no-Kami}}, {{jalink|Sokotsutsuno-o-no-Kami}})
*Yamamuro Shrine (deities: {{jalink|Ōmononushi-no-Kami}}, {{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane-kō}})
*Toyoho Shrine (deities: {{jalink|Ōhirume-no-Mikoto||Ōhirume-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Toyoukehime-no-Kami||Toyoukehime-no-Kami}}, Ame-no-Uzume-no-Kami)
*Kōjin Shrine (deities: {{jalink|Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami}}, Niwatakatsuhi-no-Kami)
*Hirata Shrine (deities: {{jalink|Sarutahiko-no-Kami}}, {{jalink|Ōnamuchi-no-Kami}}, {{jalink|Yae-Kotoshironushi-no-Kami}})

=== Access ===
*2 km west from {{jalink|Kujō Station (Nara)|Q6442373|Kujō Station}} on the {{jalink|Kintetsu Kashihara Line|Q2491066}} (about 20 minutes on foot)

*Parking available

'''Tomi Shrine''' (とみじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=Engishiki Jinmeichō |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref> {{jalink|enshrined|Q927365}} in Ishiki-chō, {{jalink|Nara|Q169134}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. Its former shrine rank was {{jalink|prefectural shrine}} (ranked in {{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 14). Ritual {{jalink|earthenware|Q1377111}} has been excavated from Myōjin-yama north of the shrine.

According to tradition, the origin is that on Kōki 4 (spring, February 23), {{jalink|Emperor Jinmu|Q200188}} worshiped the {{jalink|Imperial ancestral deities|Q11581090|imperial heavenly ancestors}} (or {{jalink|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto}}) at this place. Later, the Tomi-no-Muraji clan enshrined Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and his consort in this area where their ancestor had resided. Including smaller shrines, 22 deities are worshiped<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://ntomishrine.wixsite.com/official |title=Tomi Shrine |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref>.

=== Enshrined deities ===
The honden faces south, with western and eastern honden side by side. Deities of the western honden are {{jalink|Kamimusubi-no-Kami}}, Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto, and {{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508}}; deities of the eastern honden are {{jalink|Takamimusubi-no-Kami}} and {{jalink|Honda-wake-no-Mikoto}}. Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto is also called Tomi Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and Kijima Daimyōjin.

=== Cultural properties ===
*On April 3, Reiwa 2, the honden, haiden, shinsenjo, chōzuya, and shrine office were registered as national tangible cultural properties by the Agency for Cultural Affairs.
*{{jalink|Kayu uranai}} was designated an intangible folk cultural property of Nara City on March 1, Shōwa 57.
=== Precinct shrines ===
*Haraedo Shrine (deities: {{jalink|Seoritsuhime-no-Kami||Seoritsuhime-no-Kami}}, {{jalink|Hayakitsuhime-no-Kami}}, {{jalink|Ibukido-nushi-no-Kami|Q86734190}}, {{jalink|Hayasasurahime-no-Kami|Q136731213}}, {{jalink|Uwatsutsuno-o-no-Kami}}, {{jalink|Nakatsutsuno-o-no-Kami}}, {{jalink|Sokotsutsuno-o-no-Kami}})
*Yamamuro Shrine (deities: {{jalink|Ōmononushi-no-Kami}}, {{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane-kō}})
*Toyoho Shrine (deities: {{jalink|Ōhirume-no-Mikoto||Ōhirume-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Toyoukehime-no-Kami||Toyoukehime-no-Kami}}, Ame-no-Uzume-no-Kami)
*Kōjin Shrine (deities: {{jalink|Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami}}, Niwatakatsuhi-no-Kami)
*Hirata Shrine (deities: {{jalink|Sarutahiko-no-Kami}}, {{jalink|Ōnamuchi-no-Kami}}, {{jalink|Yae-Kotoshironushi-no-Kami}})

=== Access ===
*2 km west from {{jalink|Kujō Station (Nara)|Q6442373|Kujō Station}} on the {{jalink|Kintetsu Kashihara Line|Q2491066}} (about 20 minutes on foot)

*Parking available

'''Tomi Shrine''' (とみじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=Engishiki Jinmeichō |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref> {{jalink|enshrined|Q927365}} in Ishiki-chō, {{jalink|Nara|Q169134}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. Its former shrine rank was {{jalink|prefectural shrine}} (ranked in {{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 14). Ritual {{jalink|earthenware|Q1377111}} has been excavated from Myōjin-yama north of the shrine.

According to tradition, the origin is that on Kōki 4 (spring, February 23), {{jalink|Emperor Jinmu|Q200188}} worshiped the {{jalink|Imperial ancestral deities|Q11581090|imperial heavenly ancestors}} (or {{jalink|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto}}) at this place. Later, the Tomi-no-Muraji clan enshrined Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and his consort in this area where their ancestor had resided. Including smaller shrines, 22 deities are worshiped<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://ntomishrine.wixsite.com/official |title=Tomi Shrine |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref>.

=== Enshrined deities ===
The honden faces south, with western and eastern honden side by side. Deities of the western honden are {{jalink|Kamimusubi-no-Kami}}, Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto, and {{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508}}; deities of the eastern honden are {{jalink|Takamimusubi-no-Kami}} and {{jalink|Honda-wake-no-Mikoto}}. Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto is also called Tomi Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and Kijima Daimyōjin.

=== Cultural properties ===
*On April 3, Reiwa 2, the honden, haiden, shinsenjo, chōzuya, and shrine office were registered as national tangible cultural properties by the Agency for Cultural Affairs.
*{{jalink|Kayu uranai}} was designated an intangible folk cultural property of Nara City on March 1, Shōwa 57.
=== Precinct shrines ===
*Haraedo Shrine (deities: {{jalink|Seoritsuhime-no-Kami||Seoritsuhime-no-Kami}}, {{jalink|Hayakitsuhime-no-Kami}}, {{jalink|Ibukido-nushi-no-Kami|Q86734190}}, {{jalink|Hayasasurahime-no-Kami|Q136731213}}, {{jalink|Uwatsutsuno-o-no-Kami}}, {{jalink|Nakatsutsuno-o-no-Kami}}, {{jalink|Sokotsutsuno-o-no-Kami}})
*Yamamuro Shrine (deities: {{jalink|Ōmononushi-no-Kami}}, {{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane-kō}})
*Toyoho Shrine (deities: {{jalink|Ōhirume-no-Mikoto||Ōhirume-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Toyoukehime-no-Kami||Toyoukehime-no-Kami}}, Ame-no-Uzume-no-Kami)
*Kōjin Shrine (deities: {{jalink|Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami}}, Niwatakatsuhi-no-Kami)
*Hirata Shrine (deities: {{jalink|Sarutahiko-no-Kami}}, {{jalink|Ōnamuchi-no-Kami}}, {{jalink|Yae-Kotoshironushi-no-Kami}})

=== Access ===
*2 km west from {{jalink|Kujō Station (Nara)|Q6442373|Kujō Station}} on the {{jalink|Kintetsu Kashihara Line|Q2491066}} (about 20 minutes on foot)

*Parking available

'''Tomi Shrine''' (とみじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=Engishiki Jinmeichō |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref> {{jalink|enshrined|Q927365}} in Ishiki-chō, {{jalink|Nara|Q169134}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. Its former shrine rank was {{jalink|prefectural shrine}} (ranked in {{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 14). Ritual {{jalink|earthenware|Q1377111}} has been excavated from Myōjin-yama north of the shrine.

According to tradition, the origin is that on Kōki 4 (spring, February 23), {{jalink|Emperor Jinmu|Q200188}} worshiped the {{jalink|Imperial ancestral deities|Q11581090|imperial heavenly ancestors}} (or {{jalink|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto}}) at this place. Later, the Tomi-no-Muraji clan enshrined Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and his consort in this area where their ancestor had resided. Including smaller shrines, 22 deities are worshiped<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://ntomishrine.wixsite.com/official |title=Tomi Shrine |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref>.

=== Enshrined deities ===
The honden faces south, with western and eastern honden side by side. Deities of the western honden are {{jalink|Kamimusubi-no-Kami}}, Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto, and {{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508}}; deities of the eastern honden are {{jalink|Takamimusubi-no-Kami}} and {{jalink|Honda-wake-no-Mikoto}}. Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto is also called Tomi Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and Kijima Daimyōjin.

=== Cultural properties ===
*On April 3, Reiwa 2, the honden, haiden, shinsenjo, chōzuya, and shrine office were registered as national tangible cultural properties by the Agency for Cultural Affairs.
*{{jalink|Kayu uranai}} was designated an intangible folk cultural property of Nara City on March 1, Shōwa 57.
=== Precinct shrines ===
*Haraedo Shrine (deities: {{jalink|Seoritsuhime-no-Kami||Seoritsuhime-no-Kami}}, {{jalink|Hayakitsuhime-no-Kami}}, {{jalink|Ibukido-nushi-no-Kami|Q86734190}}, {{jalink|Hayasasurahime-no-Kami|Q136731213}}, {{jalink|Uwatsutsuno-o-no-Kami}}, {{jalink|Nakatsutsuno-o-no-Kami}}, {{jalink|Sokotsutsuno-o-no-Kami}})
*Yamamuro Shrine (deities: {{jalink|Ōmononushi-no-Kami}}, {{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane-kō}})
*Toyoho Shrine (deities: {{jalink|Ōhirume-no-Mikoto||Ōhirume-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Toyoukehime-no-Kami||Toyoukehime-no-Kami}}, Ame-no-Uzume-no-Kami)
*Kōjin Shrine (deities: {{jalink|Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami}}, Niwatakatsuhi-no-Kami)
*Hirata Shrine (deities: {{jalink|Sarutahiko-no-Kami}}, {{jalink|Ōnamuchi-no-Kami}}, {{jalink|Yae-Kotoshironushi-no-Kami}})

=== Access ===
*2 km west from {{jalink|Kujō Station (Nara)|Q6442373|Kujō Station}} on the {{jalink|Kintetsu Kashihara Line|Q2491066}} (about 20 minutes on foot)

*Parking available

{{nihongo|'''Tomi Shrine'''|登彌神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

'''Tomi Shrine''' (とみじんじゃ) is a {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=Engishiki Jinmeichō |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref> {{ill|lt=enshrined|Q927365|WD=Q927365}} in Ishiki-chō, {{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. Its former shrine rank was {{ill||lt=prefectural shrine|UNKNOWN}} (ranked in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 14). Ritual {{ill|lt=earthenware|Q1377111|WD=Q1377111}} has been excavated from Myōjin-yama north of the shrine.

According to tradition, the origin is that on Kōki 4 (spring, February 23), {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188}} worshiped the {{ill|lt=Imperial ancestral deities|Q11581090|WD=Q11581090|lt=imperial heavenly ancestors}} (or {{ill||lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}) at this place. Later, the Tomi-no-Muraji clan enshrined Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and his consort in this area where their ancestor had resided. Including smaller shrines, 22 deities are worshiped<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://ntomishrine.wixsite.com/official |title=Tomi Shrine |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref>.

=== Enshrined deities ===
The honden faces south, with western and eastern honden side by side. Deities of the western honden are {{ill||lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|UNKNOWN}}, Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto, and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508}}; deities of the eastern honden are {{ill||lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|UNKNOWN}} and {{ill||lt=Honda-wake-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}. Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto is also called Tomi Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and Kijima Daimyōjin.

=== Cultural properties ===
*On April 3, Reiwa 2, the honden, haiden, shinsenjo, chōzuya, and shrine office were registered as national tangible cultural properties by the Agency for Cultural Affairs.
*{{ill||lt=Kayu uranai|UNKNOWN}} was designated an intangible folk cultural property of Nara City on March 1, Shōwa 57.
=== Precinct shrines ===
*Haraedo Shrine (deities: {{ill|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Kami||WD=|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Kami}}, {{ill||lt=Hayakitsuhime-no-Kami|UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Ibukido-nushi-no-Kami|Q86734190|WD=Q86734190}}, {{ill|lt=Hayasasurahime-no-Kami|Q136731213|WD=Q136731213}}, {{ill||lt=Uwatsutsuno-o-no-Kami|UNKNOWN}}, {{ill||lt=Nakatsutsuno-o-no-Kami|UNKNOWN}}, {{ill||lt=Sokotsutsuno-o-no-Kami|UNKNOWN}})
*Yamamuro Shrine (deities: {{ill||lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|UNKNOWN}}, {{ill||lt=Sugawara no Michizane-kō|UNKNOWN}})
*Toyoho Shrine (deities: {{ill|lt=Ōhirume-no-Mikoto||WD=|lt=Ōhirume-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Toyoukehime-no-Kami||WD=|lt=Toyoukehime-no-Kami}}, Ame-no-Uzume-no-Kami)
*Kōjin Shrine (deities: {{ill||lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami|UNKNOWN}}, Niwatakatsuhi-no-Kami)
*Hirata Shrine (deities: {{ill||lt=Sarutahiko-no-Kami|UNKNOWN}}, {{ill||lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Kami|UNKNOWN}}, {{ill||lt=Yae-Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|UNKNOWN}})

=== Access ===
*2 km west from {{ill|lt=Kujō Station (Nara)|Q6442373|WD=Q6442373|lt=Kujō Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Kashihara Line|Q2491066|WD=Q2491066}} (about 20 minutes on foot)

*Parking available

{{nihongo|'''Tomi Shrine'''|登彌神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

'''Tomi Shrine''' (とみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=Engishiki Jinmeichō |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref> {{ill|lt=enshrined|Q927365|WD=Q927365}} in Ishiki-chō, {{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|UNKNOWN}} (ranked in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 14). Ritual {{ill|lt=earthenware|Q1377111|WD=Q1377111}} has been excavated from Myōjin-yama north of the shrine.

According to tradition, the origin is that on Kōki 4 (spring, February 23), {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188}} worshiped the {{ill|lt=Imperial ancestral deities|Q11581090|WD=Q11581090|lt=imperial heavenly ancestors}} (or {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}) at this place. Later, the Tomi-no-Muraji clan enshrined Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and his consort in this area where their ancestor had resided. Including smaller shrines, 22 deities are worshiped<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://ntomishrine.wixsite.com/official |title=Tomi Shrine |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref>.

=== Enshrined deities ===
The honden faces south, with western and eastern honden side by side. Deities of the western honden are {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|UNKNOWN}}, Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto, and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508}}; deities of the eastern honden are {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Honda-wake-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}. Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto is also called Tomi Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and Kijima Daimyōjin.

=== Cultural properties ===
*On April 3, Reiwa 2, the honden, haiden, shinsenjo, chōzuya, and shrine office were registered as national tangible cultural properties by the Agency for Cultural Affairs.
*{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kayu uranai|UNKNOWN}} was designated an intangible folk cultural property of Nara City on March 1, Shōwa 57.
=== Precinct shrines ===
*Haraedo Shrine (deities: {{ill|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Kami||WD=|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Kami}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hayakitsuhime-no-Kami|UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Ibukido-nushi-no-Kami|Q86734190|WD=Q86734190}}, {{ill|lt=Hayasasurahime-no-Kami|Q136731213|WD=Q136731213}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Uwatsutsuno-o-no-Kami|UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakatsutsuno-o-no-Kami|UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sokotsutsuno-o-no-Kami|UNKNOWN}})
*Yamamuro Shrine (deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sugawara no Michizane-kō|UNKNOWN}})
*Toyoho Shrine (deities: {{ill|lt=Ōhirume-no-Mikoto||WD=|lt=Ōhirume-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Toyoukehime-no-Kami||WD=|lt=Toyoukehime-no-Kami}}, Ame-no-Uzume-no-Kami)
*Kōjin Shrine (deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami|UNKNOWN}}, Niwatakatsuhi-no-Kami)
*Hirata Shrine (deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Kami|UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Kami|UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yae-Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|UNKNOWN}})

=== Access ===
*2 km west from {{ill|lt=Kujō Station (Nara)|Q6442373|WD=Q6442373|lt=Kujō Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Kashihara Line|Q2491066|WD=Q2491066}} (about 20 minutes on foot)

*Parking available

{{nihongo|'''Tomi Shrine'''|登彌神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

== Japanese content ==
{{混同|For the shrine in Sakurai, Nara|{{ill|lt=Tomi Shrine|Q11602724|WD=Q11602724}}}}

'''Tomi Shrine''' (とみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=Engishiki Jinmeichō |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref> {{ill|lt=enshrined|Q927365|WD=Q927365}} in Ishiki-chō, {{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (ranked in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 14). Ritual {{ill|lt=earthenware|Q1377111|WD=Q1377111}} has been excavated from Myōjin-yama north of the shrine.

According to tradition, the origin is that on Kōki 4 (spring, February 23), {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188}} worshiped the {{ill|lt=Imperial ancestral deities|Q11581090|WD=Q11581090|lt=imperial heavenly ancestors}} (or {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}) at this place. Later, the Tomi-no-Muraji clan enshrined Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and his consort in this area where their ancestor had resided. Including smaller shrines, 22 deities are worshiped<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://ntomishrine.wixsite.com/official |title=Tomi Shrine |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref>.

=== Enshrined deities ===
The honden faces south, with western and eastern honden side by side. Deities of the western honden are {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto, and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508}}; deities of the eastern honden are {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Honda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto is also called Tomi Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and Kijima Daimyōjin.

=== Cultural properties ===
*On April 3, Reiwa 2, the honden, haiden, shinsenjo, chōzuya, and shrine office were registered as national tangible cultural properties by the Agency for Cultural Affairs.
*{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kayu uranai|1=UNKNOWN}} was designated an intangible folk cultural property of Nara City on March 1, Shōwa 57.
=== Precinct shrines ===
*Haraedo Shrine (deities: {{ill|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Kami||WD=|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Kami}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hayakitsuhime-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Ibukido-nushi-no-Kami|Q86734190|WD=Q86734190}}, {{ill|lt=Hayasasurahime-no-Kami|Q136731213|WD=Q136731213}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Uwatsutsuno-o-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakatsutsuno-o-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sokotsutsuno-o-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}})
*Yamamuro Shrine (deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sugawara no Michizane-kō|1=UNKNOWN}})
*Toyoho Shrine (deities: {{ill|lt=Ōhirume-no-Mikoto||WD=|lt=Ōhirume-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Toyoukehime-no-Kami||WD=|lt=Toyoukehime-no-Kami}}, Ame-no-Uzume-no-Kami)
*Kōjin Shrine (deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, Niwatakatsuhi-no-Kami)
*Hirata Shrine (deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yae-Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}})

=== Access ===
*2 km west from {{ill|lt=Kujō Station (Nara)|Q6442373|WD=Q6442373|lt=Kujō Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Kashihara Line|Q2491066|WD=Q2491066}} (about 20 minutes on foot)

*Parking available

'''Tomi Shrine''' (とみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=Engishiki Jinmeichō |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref> {{ill|lt=enshrined|Q927365|WD=Q927365}} in Ishiki-chō, {{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (ranked in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 14). Ritual {{ill|lt=earthenware|Q1377111|WD=Q1377111}} has been excavated from Myōjin-yama north of the shrine.

According to tradition, the origin is that on Kōki 4 (spring, February 23), {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188}} worshiped the {{ill|lt=Imperial ancestral deities|Q11581090|WD=Q11581090|lt=imperial heavenly ancestors}} (or {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}) at this place. Later, the Tomi-no-Muraji clan enshrined Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and his consort in this area where their ancestor had resided. Including smaller shrines, 22 deities are worshiped<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://ntomishrine.wixsite.com/official |title=Tomi Shrine |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref>.

=== Enshrined deities ===
The honden faces south, with western and eastern honden side by side. Deities of the western honden are {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto, and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508}}; deities of the eastern honden are {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Honda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto is also called Tomi Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and Kijima Daimyōjin.

=== Cultural properties ===
*On April 3, Reiwa 2, the honden, haiden, shinsenjo, chōzuya, and shrine office were registered as national tangible cultural properties by the Agency for Cultural Affairs.
*{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kayu uranai|1=UNKNOWN}} was designated an intangible folk cultural property of Nara City on March 1, Shōwa 57.
=== Precinct shrines ===
*Haraedo Shrine (deities: {{ill|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|WD=|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Kami}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hayakitsuhime-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Ibukido-nushi-no-Kami|Q86734190|WD=Q86734190}}, {{ill|lt=Hayasasurahime-no-Kami|Q136731213|WD=Q136731213}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Uwatsutsuno-o-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakatsutsuno-o-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sokotsutsuno-o-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}})
*Yamamuro Shrine (deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sugawara no Michizane-kō|1=UNKNOWN}})
*Toyoho Shrine (deities: {{ill|lt=Ōhirume-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|WD=|lt=Ōhirume-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Toyoukehime-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|WD=|lt=Toyoukehime-no-Kami}}, Ame-no-Uzume-no-Kami)
*Kōjin Shrine (deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, Niwatakatsuhi-no-Kami)
*Hirata Shrine (deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yae-Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}})

=== Access ===
*2 km west from {{ill|lt=Kujō Station (Nara)|Q6442373|WD=Q6442373|lt=Kujō Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Kashihara Line|Q2491066|WD=Q2491066}} (about 20 minutes on foot)

*Parking available

{{nihongo|'''Tomi Shrine'''|登彌神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

'''Tomi Shrine''' (とみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=Engishiki Jinmeichō |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref> {{ill|lt=enshrined|Q927365|WD=Q927365}} in Ishiki-chō, {{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (ranked in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 14). Ritual {{ill|lt=earthenware|Q1377111|WD=Q1377111}} has been excavated from Myōjin-yama north of the shrine.

According to tradition, the origin is that on Kōki 4 (spring, February 23), {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188}} worshiped the {{ill|lt=Imperial ancestral deities|Q11581090|WD=Q11581090|lt=imperial heavenly ancestors}} (or {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}) at this place. Later, the Tomi-no-Muraji clan enshrined Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and his consort in this area where their ancestor had resided. Including smaller shrines, 22 deities are worshiped<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://ntomishrine.wixsite.com/official |title=Tomi Shrine |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref>.

=== Enshrined deities ===
The honden faces south, with western and eastern honden side by side. Deities of the western honden are {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto, and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508}}; deities of the eastern honden are {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Honda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto is also called Tomi Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and Kijima Daimyōjin.

=== Cultural properties ===
*On April 3, Reiwa 2, the honden, haiden, shinsenjo, chōzuya, and shrine office were registered as national tangible cultural properties by the Agency for Cultural Affairs.
*{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kayu uranai|1=UNKNOWN}} was designated an intangible folk cultural property of Nara City on March 1, Shōwa 57.
=== Precinct shrines ===
*Haraedo Shrine (deities: {{ill|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Kami}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hayakitsuhime-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Ibukido-nushi-no-Kami|Q86734190|WD=Q86734190}}, {{ill|lt=Hayasasurahime-no-Kami|Q136731213|WD=Q136731213}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Uwatsutsuno-o-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakatsutsuno-o-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sokotsutsuno-o-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}})
*Yamamuro Shrine (deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sugawara no Michizane-kō|1=UNKNOWN}})
*Toyoho Shrine (deities: {{ill|lt=Ōhirume-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōhirume-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Toyoukehime-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyoukehime-no-Kami}}, Ame-no-Uzume-no-Kami)
*Kōjin Shrine (deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, Niwatakatsuhi-no-Kami)
*Hirata Shrine (deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yae-Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}})

=== Access ===
*2 km west from {{ill|lt=Kujō Station (Nara)|Q6442373|WD=Q6442373|lt=Kujō Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Kashihara Line|Q2491066|WD=Q2491066}} (about 20 minutes on foot)

*Parking available

{{nihongo|'''Tomi Shrine'''|登彌神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

'''Tomi Shrine''' (とみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=Engishiki Jinmeichō |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref> {{ill|lt=enshrined|Q927365|WD=Q927365}} in Ishiki-chō, {{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (ranked in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 14). Ritual {{ill|lt=earthenware|Q1377111|WD=Q1377111}} has been excavated from Myōjin-yama north of the shrine.

According to tradition, the origin is that on Kōki 4 (spring, February 23), {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188}} worshiped the {{ill|lt=Imperial ancestral deities|Q11581090|WD=Q11581090|lt=imperial heavenly ancestors}} (or {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}) at this place. Later, the Tomi-no-Muraji clan enshrined Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and his consort in this area where their ancestor had resided. Including smaller shrines, 22 deities are worshiped<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://ntomishrine.wixsite.com/official |title=Tomi Shrine |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref>.

=== Enshrined deities ===
The honden faces south, with western and eastern honden side by side. Deities of the western honden are {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto, and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508}}; deities of the eastern honden are {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Honda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto is also called Tomi Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and Kijima Daimyōjin.

=== Cultural properties ===
*On April 3, Reiwa 2, the honden, haiden, shinsenjo, chōzuya, and shrine office were registered as national tangible cultural properties by the Agency for Cultural Affairs.
*{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kayu uranai|1=UNKNOWN}} was designated an intangible folk cultural property of Nara City on March 1, Shōwa 57.
=== Precinct shrines ===
*Haraedo Shrine (deities: {{ill|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Kami}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hayakitsuhime-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Ibukido-nushi-no-Kami|Q86734190|WD=Q86734190}}, {{ill|lt=Hayasasurahime-no-Kami|Q136731213|WD=Q136731213}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Uwatsutsuno-o-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakatsutsuno-o-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sokotsutsuno-o-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}})
*Yamamuro Shrine (deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sugawara no Michizane-kō|1=UNKNOWN}})
*Toyoho Shrine (deities: {{ill|lt=Ōhirume-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōhirume-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Toyoukehime-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyoukehime-no-Kami}}, Ame-no-Uzume-no-Kami)
*Kōjin Shrine (deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, Niwatakatsuhi-no-Kami)
*Hirata Shrine (deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yae-Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}})

=== Access ===
*2 km west from {{ill|lt=Kujō Station (Nara)|Q6442373|WD=Q6442373|lt=Kujō Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Kashihara Line|Q2491066|WD=Q2491066}} (about 20 minutes on foot)

*Parking available

{{nihongo|'''Tomi Shrine'''|登彌神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

'''Tomi Shrine''' (とみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=Engishiki Jinmeichō |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref> {{ill|lt=enshrined|Q927365|WD=Q927365}} in Ishiki-chō, {{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}} (ranked in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 14). Ritual {{ill|lt=earthenware|Q1377111|WD=Q1377111}} has been excavated from Myōjin-yama north of the shrine.

According to tradition, the origin is that on Kōki 4 (spring, February 23), {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188}} worshiped the {{ill|lt=Imperial ancestral deities|Q11581090|WD=Q11581090|lt=imperial heavenly ancestors}} (or {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}) at this place. Later, the Tomi-no-Muraji clan enshrined Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and his consort in this area where their ancestor had resided. Including smaller shrines, 22 deities are worshiped<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://ntomishrine.wixsite.com/official |title=Tomi Shrine |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref>.

=== Enshrined deities ===
The honden faces south, with western and eastern honden side by side. Deities of the western honden are {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto, and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508}}; deities of the eastern honden are {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Honda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto is also called Tomi Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and Kijima Daimyōjin.

=== Cultural properties ===
*On April 3, Reiwa 2, the honden, haiden, shinsenjo, chōzuya, and shrine office were registered as national tangible cultural properties by the Agency for Cultural Affairs.
*{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kayu uranai|1=UNKNOWN}} was designated an intangible folk cultural property of Nara City on March 1, Shōwa 57.
=== Precinct shrines ===
*Haraedo Shrine (deities: {{ill|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Kami}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hayakitsuhime-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Ibukido-nushi-no-Kami|Q86734190|WD=Q86734190}}, {{ill|lt=Hayasasurahime-no-Kami|Q136731213|WD=Q136731213}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Uwatsutsuno-o-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakatsutsuno-o-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sokotsutsuno-o-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}})
*Yamamuro Shrine (deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sugawara no Michizane-kō|1=UNKNOWN}})
*Toyoho Shrine (deities: {{ill|lt=Ōhirume-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōhirume-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Toyoukehime-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyoukehime-no-Kami}}, Ame-no-Uzume-no-Kami)
*Kōjin Shrine (deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, Niwatakatsuhi-no-Kami)
*Hirata Shrine (deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yae-Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}})

=== Access ===
*2 km west from {{ill|lt=Kujō Station (Nara)|Q6442373|WD=Q6442373|lt=Kujō Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Kashihara Line|Q2491066|WD=Q2491066}} (about 20 minutes on foot)

*Parking available

'''Tomi Shrine''' (とみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=Engishiki Jinmeichō |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref> {{ill|lt=enshrined|Q927365|WD=Q927365}} in Ishiki-chō, {{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}} (ranked in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 14). Ritual {{ill|lt=earthenware|Q1377111|WD=Q1377111}} has been excavated from Myōjin-yama north of the shrine.

According to tradition, the origin is that on Kōki 4 (spring, February 23), {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188}} worshiped the {{ill|lt=Imperial ancestral deities|Q11581090|WD=Q11581090|lt=imperial heavenly ancestors}} (or {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}) at this place. Later, the Tomi-no-Muraji clan enshrined Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and his consort in this area where their ancestor had resided. Including smaller shrines, 22 deities are worshiped<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://ntomishrine.wixsite.com/official |title=Tomi Shrine |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref>.

=== Enshrined deities ===
The honden faces south, with western and eastern honden side by side. Deities of the western honden are {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto, and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508}}; deities of the eastern honden are {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Honda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto is also called Tomi Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and Kijima Daimyōjin.

=== Cultural properties ===
*On April 3, Reiwa 2, the honden, haiden, shinsenjo, chōzuya, and shrine office were registered as national tangible cultural properties by the Agency for Cultural Affairs.
*{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kayu uranai|1=UNKNOWN}} was designated an intangible folk cultural property of Nara City on March 1, Shōwa 57.
=== Precinct shrines ===
*Haraedo Shrine (deities: {{ill|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Kami}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hayakitsuhime-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Ibukido-nushi-no-Kami|Q86734190|WD=Q86734190}}, {{ill|lt=Hayasasurahime-no-Kami|Q136731213|WD=Q136731213}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Uwatsutsuno-o-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakatsutsuno-o-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sokotsutsuno-o-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}})
*Yamamuro Shrine (deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sugawara no Michizane-kō|1=UNKNOWN}})
*Toyoho Shrine (deities: {{ill|lt=Ōhirume-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōhirume-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Toyoukehime-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyoukehime-no-Kami}}, Ame-no-Uzume-no-Kami)
*Kōjin Shrine (deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, Niwatakatsuhi-no-Kami)
*Hirata Shrine (deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q3090037}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yae-Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}})

=== Access ===
*2 km west from {{ill|lt=Kujō Station (Nara)|Q6442373|WD=Q6442373|lt=Kujō Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Kashihara Line|Q2491066|WD=Q2491066}} (about 20 minutes on foot)

*Parking available

'''Tomi Shrine''' (とみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=Engishiki Jinmeichō |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref> {{ill|lt=enshrined|Q927365|WD=Q927365}} in Ishiki-chō, {{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}} (ranked in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 14). Ritual {{ill|lt=earthenware|Q1377111|WD=Q1377111}} has been excavated from Myōjin-yama north of the shrine.

According to tradition, the origin is that on Kōki 4 (spring, February 23), {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188}} worshiped the {{ill|lt=Imperial ancestral deities|Q11581090|WD=Q11581090|lt=imperial heavenly ancestors}} (or {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}) at this place. Later, the Tomi-no-Muraji clan enshrined Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and his consort in this area where their ancestor had resided. Including smaller shrines, 22 deities are worshiped<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://ntomishrine.wixsite.com/official |title=Tomi Shrine |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref>.

=== Enshrined deities ===
The honden faces south, with western and eastern honden side by side. Deities of the western honden are {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto, and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508}}; deities of the eastern honden are {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Honda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto is also called Tomi Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and Kijima Daimyōjin.

=== Cultural properties ===
*On April 3, Reiwa 2, the honden, haiden, shinsenjo, chōzuya, and shrine office were registered as national tangible cultural properties by the Agency for Cultural Affairs.
*{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kayu uranai|1=UNKNOWN}} was designated an intangible folk cultural property of Nara City on March 1, Shōwa 57.
=== Precinct shrines ===
*Haraedo Shrine (deities: {{ill|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Kami}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hayakitsuhime-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Ibukido-nushi-no-Kami|Q86734190|WD=Q86734190}}, {{ill|lt=Hayasasurahime-no-Kami|Q136731213|WD=Q136731213}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Uwatsutsuno-o-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakatsutsuno-o-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sokotsutsuno-o-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}})
*Yamamuro Shrine (deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sugawara no Michizane-kō|1=UNKNOWN}})
*Toyoho Shrine (deities: {{ill|lt=Ōhirume-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōhirume-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Toyoukehime-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyoukehime-no-Kami}}, Ame-no-Uzume-no-Kami)
*Kōjin Shrine (deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, Niwatakatsuhi-no-Kami)
*Hirata Shrine (deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q3090037}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yae-Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}})

=== Access ===
*2 km west from {{ill|lt=Kujō Station (Nara)|Q6442373|WD=Q6442373|lt=Kujō Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Kashihara Line|Q2491066|WD=Q2491066}} (about 20 minutes on foot)

*Parking available

'''Tomi Shrine''' (とみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=Engishiki Jinmeichō |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref> {{ill|lt=enshrined|Q927365|WD=Q927365}} in Ishiki-chō, {{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}} (ranked in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 14). Ritual {{ill|lt=earthenware|Q1377111|WD=Q1377111}} has been excavated from Myōjin-yama north of the shrine.

According to tradition, the origin is that on Kōki 4 (spring, February 23), {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188}} worshiped the {{ill|lt=Imperial ancestral deities|Q11581090|WD=Q11581090|lt=imperial heavenly ancestors}} (or {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}) at this place. Later, the Tomi-no-Muraji clan enshrined Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and his consort in this area where their ancestor had resided. Including smaller shrines, 22 deities are worshiped<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://ntomishrine.wixsite.com/official |title=Tomi Shrine |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref>.

=== Enshrined deities ===
The honden faces south, with western and eastern honden side by side. Deities of the western honden are {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto, and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508}}; deities of the eastern honden are {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Honda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto is also called Tomi Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and Kijima Daimyōjin.

=== Cultural properties ===
*On April 3, Reiwa 2, the honden, haiden, shinsenjo, chōzuya, and shrine office were registered as national tangible cultural properties by the Agency for Cultural Affairs.
*{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kayu uranai|1=UNKNOWN}} was designated an intangible folk cultural property of Nara City on March 1, Shōwa 57.
=== Precinct shrines ===
*Haraedo Shrine (deities: {{ill|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Kami}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hayakitsuhime-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Ibukido-nushi-no-Kami|Q86734190|WD=Q86734190}}, {{ill|lt=Hayasasurahime-no-Kami|Q136731213|WD=Q136731213}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Uwatsutsuno-o-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakatsutsuno-o-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sokotsutsuno-o-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}})
*Yamamuro Shrine (deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sugawara no Michizane-kō|1=UNKNOWN}})
*Toyoho Shrine (deities: {{ill|lt=Ōhirume-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōhirume-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Toyoukehime-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyoukehime-no-Kami}}, Ame-no-Uzume-no-Kami)
*Kōjin Shrine (deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, Niwatakatsuhi-no-Kami)
*Hirata Shrine (deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q3090037}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yae-Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}})

=== Access ===
*2 km west from {{ill|lt=Kujō Station (Nara)|Q6442373|WD=Q6442373|lt=Kujō Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Kashihara Line|Q2491066|WD=Q2491066}} (about 20 minutes on foot)

*Parking available

{{nihongo|'''Tomi Shrine'''|登彌神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

'''Tomi Shrine''' (とみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=Engishiki Jinmeichō |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref> {{ill|lt=enshrined|Q927365|WD=Q927365}} in Ishiki-chō, {{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}} (ranked in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 14). Ritual {{ill|lt=earthenware|Q1377111|WD=Q1377111}} has been excavated from Myōjin-yama north of the shrine.

According to tradition, the origin is that on Kōki 4 (spring, February 23), {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188}} worshiped the {{ill|lt=Imperial ancestral deities|Q11581090|WD=Q11581090|lt=imperial heavenly ancestors}} (or {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}) at this place. Later, the Tomi-no-Muraji clan enshrined Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and his consort in this area where their ancestor had resided. Including smaller shrines, 22 deities are worshiped<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://ntomishrine.wixsite.com/official |title=Tomi Shrine |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref>.

=== Enshrined deities ===
The honden faces south, with western and eastern honden side by side. Deities of the western honden are {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto, and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508}}; deities of the eastern honden are {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Honda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto is also called Tomi Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and Kijima Daimyōjin.

=== Cultural properties ===
*On April 3, Reiwa 2, the honden, haiden, shinsenjo, chōzuya, and shrine office were registered as national tangible cultural properties by the Agency for Cultural Affairs.
*{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kayu uranai|1=UNKNOWN}} was designated an intangible folk cultural property of Nara City on March 1, Shōwa 57.
=== Precinct shrines ===
*Haraedo Shrine (deities: {{ill|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Kami}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hayakitsuhime-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Ibukido-nushi-no-Kami|Q86734190|WD=Q86734190}}, {{ill|lt=Hayasasurahime-no-Kami|Q136731213|WD=Q136731213}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Uwatsutsuno-o-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakatsutsuno-o-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sokotsutsuno-o-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}})
*Yamamuro Shrine (deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sugawara no Michizane-kō|1=UNKNOWN}})
*Toyoho Shrine (deities: {{ill|lt=Ōhirume-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōhirume-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Toyoukehime-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyoukehime-no-Kami}}, Ame-no-Uzume-no-Kami)
*Kōjin Shrine (deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, Niwatakatsuhi-no-Kami)
*Hirata Shrine (deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q3090037}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yae-Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}})

=== Access ===
*2 km west from {{ill|lt=Kujō Station (Nara)|Q6442373|WD=Q6442373|lt=Kujō Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Kashihara Line|Q2491066|WD=Q2491066}} (about 20 minutes on foot)

*Parking available

{{nihongo|'''Tomi Shrine'''|登彌神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].
