'''Tomi Shrine''' (とみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=Engishiki Jinmeichō |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref> {{ill|lt=enshrined|Q927365|WD=Q927365}} in Ishiki-chō, {{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}} (ranked in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 14). Ritual {{ill|lt=earthenware|Q1377111|WD=Q1377111}} has been excavated from Myōjin-yama north of the shrine.

According to tradition, the origin is that on Kōki 4 (spring, February 23), {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188}} worshiped the {{ill|lt=Imperial ancestral deities|Q11581090|WD=Q11581090|lt=imperial heavenly ancestors}} (or {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}) at this place. Later, the Tomi-no-Muraji clan enshrined Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and his consort in this area where their ancestor had resided. Including smaller shrines, 22 deities are worshiped<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://ntomishrine.wixsite.com/official |title=Tomi Shrine |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref>.

=== Enshrined deities ===
The honden faces south, with western and eastern honden side by side. Deities of the western honden are {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3275901|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto, and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508}}; deities of the eastern honden are {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Honda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto is also called Tomi Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and Kijima Daimyōjin.

=== Cultural properties ===
*On April 3, Reiwa 2, the honden, haiden, shinsenjo, chōzuya, and shrine office were registered as national tangible cultural properties by the Agency for Cultural Affairs.
*{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kayu uranai|1=UNKNOWN}} was designated an intangible folk cultural property of Nara City on March 1, Shōwa 57.
=== Precinct shrines ===
*Haraedo Shrine (deities: {{ill|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Kami}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hayakitsuhime-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Ibukido-nushi-no-Kami|Q86734190|WD=Q86734190}}, {{ill|lt=Hayasasurahime-no-Kami|Q136731213|WD=Q136731213}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Uwatsutsuno-o-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakatsutsuno-o-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sokotsutsuno-o-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}})
*Yamamuro Shrine (deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sugawara no Michizane-kō|1=UNKNOWN}})
*Toyoho Shrine (deities: {{ill|lt=Ōhirume-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōhirume-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Toyoukehime-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyoukehime-no-Kami}}, Ame-no-Uzume-no-Kami)
*Kōjin Shrine (deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, Niwatakatsuhi-no-Kami)
*Hirata Shrine (deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q3090037}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yae-Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}})

=== Access ===
*2 km west from {{ill|lt=Kujō Station (Nara)|Q6442373|WD=Q6442373|lt=Kujō Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Kashihara Line|Q2491066|WD=Q2491066}} (about 20 minutes on foot)

*Parking available

{{nihongo|'''Tomi Shrine'''|登彌神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

== Japanese content ==
{{混同|For the shrine in Sakurai, Nara|{{ill|lt=Tomi Shrine|Q11602724|WD=Q11602724}}}}
{{神社
|名称    = Tomi Shrine
|画像    = {{画像募集中|cat=奈良市}}

|神体    = 
|社格    = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}}

'''Tomi Shrine''' (とみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=Engishiki Jinmeichō |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref> {{ill|lt=enshrined|Q927365|WD=Q927365}} in Ishiki-chō, {{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}} (ranked in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 14). Ritual {{ill|lt=earthenware|Q1377111|WD=Q1377111}} has been excavated from Myōjin-yama north of the shrine.

According to tradition, the origin is that on Kōki 4 (spring, February 23), {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188}} worshiped the {{ill|lt=Imperial ancestral deities|Q11581090|WD=Q11581090|lt=imperial heavenly ancestors}} (or {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}) at this place. Later, the Tomi-no-Muraji clan enshrined Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and his consort in this area where their ancestor had resided. Including smaller shrines, 22 deities are worshiped<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://ntomishrine.wixsite.com/official |title=Tomi Shrine |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref>.

=== Enshrined deities ===
The honden faces south, with western and eastern honden side by side. Deities of the western honden are {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3275901|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto, and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508}}; deities of the eastern honden are {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Honda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto is also called Tomi Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and Kijima Daimyōjin.

=== Cultural properties ===
*On April 3, Reiwa 2, the honden, haiden, shinsenjo, chōzuya, and shrine office were registered as national tangible cultural properties by the Agency for Cultural Affairs.
*{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kayu uranai|1=UNKNOWN}} was designated an intangible folk cultural property of Nara City on March 1, Shōwa 57.
=== Precinct shrines ===
*Haraedo Shrine (deities: {{ill|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Kami}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hayakitsuhime-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Ibukido-nushi-no-Kami|Q86734190|WD=Q86734190}}, {{ill|lt=Hayasasurahime-no-Kami|Q136731213|WD=Q136731213}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Uwatsutsuno-o-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakatsutsuno-o-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sokotsutsuno-o-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}})
*Yamamuro Shrine (deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sugawara no Michizane-kō|1=UNKNOWN}})
*Toyoho Shrine (deities: {{ill|lt=Ōhirume-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōhirume-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Toyoukehime-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyoukehime-no-Kami}}, Ame-no-Uzume-no-Kami)
*Kōjin Shrine (deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, Niwatakatsuhi-no-Kami)
*Hirata Shrine (deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q3090037}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yae-Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}})

=== Access ===
*2 km west from {{ill|lt=Kujō Station (Nara)|Q6442373|WD=Q6442373|lt=Kujō Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Kashihara Line|Q2491066|WD=Q2491066}} (about 20 minutes on foot)

*Parking available

'''Tomi Shrine''' (とみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=Engishiki Jinmeichō |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref> {{ill|lt=enshrined|Q927365|WD=Q927365|1=Louis de Bourbon, Duke of Montpensier}} in Ishiki-chō, {{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134|1=Nara (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} (ranked in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 14). Ritual {{ill|lt=earthenware|Q1377111|WD=Q1377111|1=Earthenware}} has been excavated from Myōjin-yama north of the shrine.

According to tradition, the origin is that on Kōki 4 (spring, February 23), {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} worshiped the {{ill|lt=Imperial ancestral deities|Q11581090|WD=Q11581090|lt=imperial heavenly ancestors|1=Ancestors of the Emperor}} (or {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}) at this place. Later, the Tomi-no-Muraji clan enshrined Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and his consort in this area where their ancestor had resided. Including smaller shrines, 22 deities are worshiped<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://ntomishrine.wixsite.com/official |title=Tomi Shrine |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref>.

=== Enshrined deities ===
The honden faces south, with western and eastern honden side by side. Deities of the western honden are {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3275901|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Kamimusubi}}, Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto, and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}; deities of the eastern honden are {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Honda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto is also called Tomi Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and Kijima Daimyōjin.

=== Cultural properties ===
*On April 3, Reiwa 2, the honden, haiden, shinsenjo, chōzuya, and shrine office were registered as national tangible cultural properties by the Agency for Cultural Affairs.
*{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kayu uranai|1=UNKNOWN}} was designated an intangible folk cultural property of Nara City on March 1, Shōwa 57.
=== Precinct shrines ===
*Haraedo Shrine (deities: {{ill|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Kami}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hayakitsuhime-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Ibukido-nushi-no-Kami|Q86734190|WD=Q86734190|1=Ibukidonushi no Kami}}, {{ill|lt=Hayasasurahime-no-Kami|Q136731213|WD=Q136731213|1=Hayasasurahime}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Uwatsutsuno-o-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakatsutsuno-o-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sokotsutsuno-o-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}})
*Yamamuro Shrine (deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|1=Ōmononushi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sugawara no Michizane-kō|1=UNKNOWN}})
*Toyoho Shrine (deities: {{ill|lt=Ōhirume-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōhirume-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Toyoukehime-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyoukehime-no-Kami}}, Ame-no-Uzume-no-Kami)
*Kōjin Shrine (deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami|1=Ōyamatsumi}}, Niwatakatsuhi-no-Kami)
*Hirata Shrine (deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Kami|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami|WD=Q3090037}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yae-Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}})

=== Access ===
*2 km west from {{ill|lt=Kujō Station (Nara)|Q6442373|WD=Q6442373|lt=Kujō Station|1=Kujō Station (Nara)}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Kashihara Line|Q2491066|WD=Q2491066|1=Kashihara Line}} (about 20 minutes on foot)

*Parking available

'''Tomi Shrine''' (とみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=Engishiki Jinmeichō |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref> {{ill|lt=enshrined|Q927365|WD=Q927365|1=Louis de Bourbon, Duke of Montpensier}} in Ishiki-chō, {{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134|1=Nara (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} (ranked in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 14). Ritual {{ill|lt=earthenware|Q1377111|WD=Q1377111|1=Earthenware}} has been excavated from Myōjin-yama north of the shrine.

According to tradition, the origin is that on Kōki 4 (spring, February 23), {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} worshiped the {{ill|lt=Imperial ancestral deities|Q11581090|WD=Q11581090|lt=imperial heavenly ancestors|1=Ancestors of the Emperor}} (or {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}) at this place. Later, the Tomi-no-Muraji clan enshrined Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and his consort in this area where their ancestor had resided. Including smaller shrines, 22 deities are worshiped<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://ntomishrine.wixsite.com/official |title=Tomi Shrine |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref>.

=== Enshrined deities ===
The honden faces south, with western and eastern honden side by side. Deities of the western honden are {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3275901|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Kamimusubi}}, Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto, and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}; deities of the eastern honden are {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Honda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto is also called Tomi Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and Kijima Daimyōjin.

=== Cultural properties ===
*On April 3, Reiwa 2, the honden, haiden, shinsenjo, chōzuya, and shrine office were registered as national tangible cultural properties by the Agency for Cultural Affairs.
*{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kayu uranai|1=UNKNOWN}} was designated an intangible folk cultural property of Nara City on March 1, Shōwa 57.
=== Precinct shrines ===
*Haraedo Shrine (deities: {{ill|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Kami}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hayakitsuhime-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Ibukido-nushi-no-Kami|Q86734190|WD=Q86734190|1=Ibukidonushi no Kami}}, {{ill|lt=Hayasasurahime-no-Kami|Q136731213|WD=Q136731213|1=Hayasasurahime}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Uwatsutsuno-o-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakatsutsuno-o-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sokotsutsuno-o-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}})
*Yamamuro Shrine (deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|1=Ōmononushi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sugawara no Michizane-kō|1=UNKNOWN}})
*Toyoho Shrine (deities: {{ill|lt=Ōhirume-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōhirume-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Toyoukehime-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyoukehime-no-Kami}}, Ame-no-Uzume-no-Kami)
*Kōjin Shrine (deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami|1=Ōyamatsumi}}, Niwatakatsuhi-no-Kami)
*Hirata Shrine (deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Kami|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami|WD=Q3090037}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yae-Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}})

=== Access ===
*2 km west from {{ill|lt=Kujō Station (Nara)|Q6442373|WD=Q6442373|lt=Kujō Station|1=Kujō Station (Nara)}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Kashihara Line|Q2491066|WD=Q2491066|1=Kashihara Line}} (about 20 minutes on foot)

*Parking available

{{nihongo|'''Tomi Shrine'''|登彌神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

'''Tomi Shrine''' (とみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=Engishiki Jinmeichō |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref> {{ill|lt=enshrined|Q927365|WD=Q927365|1=Louis de Bourbon, Duke of Montpensier}} in Ishiki-chō, {{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134|1=Nara (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} (ranked in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 14). Ritual {{ill|lt=earthenware|Q1377111|WD=Q1377111|1=Earthenware}} has been excavated from Myōjin-yama north of the shrine.

According to tradition, the origin is that on Kōki 4 (spring, February 23), {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} worshiped the {{ill|lt=Imperial ancestral deities|Q11581090|WD=Q11581090|lt=imperial heavenly ancestors|1=Ancestors of the Emperor}} (or {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}) at this place. Later, the Tomi-no-Muraji clan enshrined Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and his consort in this area where their ancestor had resided. Including smaller shrines, 22 deities are worshiped<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://ntomishrine.wixsite.com/official |title=Tomi Shrine |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref>.

=== Enshrined deities ===
The honden faces south, with western and eastern honden side by side. Deities of the western honden are {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3275901|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Kamimusubi}}, Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto, and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}; deities of the eastern honden are {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Honda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto is also called Tomi Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and Kijima Daimyōjin.

=== Cultural properties ===
*On April 3, Reiwa 2, the honden, haiden, shinsenjo, chōzuya, and shrine office were registered as national tangible cultural properties by the Agency for Cultural Affairs.
*{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kayu uranai|1=UNKNOWN}} was designated an intangible folk cultural property of Nara City on March 1, Shōwa 57.
=== Precinct shrines ===
*Haraedo Shrine (deities: {{ill|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Kami}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hayakitsuhime-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Ibukido-nushi-no-Kami|Q86734190|WD=Q86734190|1=Ibukidonushi no Kami}}, {{ill|lt=Hayasasurahime-no-Kami|Q136731213|WD=Q136731213|1=Hayasasurahime}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Uwatsutsuno-o-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakatsutsuno-o-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sokotsutsuno-o-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}})
*Yamamuro Shrine (deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|1=Ōmononushi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sugawara no Michizane-kō|1=UNKNOWN}})
*Toyoho Shrine (deities: {{ill|lt=Ōhirume-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōhirume-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Toyoukehime-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyoukehime-no-Kami}}, Ame-no-Uzume-no-Kami)
*Kōjin Shrine (deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami|1=Ōyamatsumi}}, Niwatakatsuhi-no-Kami)
*Hirata Shrine (deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Kami|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami|WD=Q3090037}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yae-Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}})

=== Access ===
*2 km west from {{ill|lt=Kujō Station (Nara)|Q6442373|WD=Q6442373|lt=Kujō Station|1=Kujō Station (Nara)}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Kashihara Line|Q2491066|WD=Q2491066|1=Kashihara Line}} (about 20 minutes on foot)

*Parking available

{{nihongo|'''Tomi Shrine'''|登彌神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

'''Tomi Shrine''' (とみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=Engishiki Jinmeichō |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref> {{ill|lt=enshrined|Q927365|WD=Q927365|1=Louis de Bourbon, Duke of Montpensier}} in Ishiki-chō, {{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134|1=Nara (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} (ranked in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 14). Ritual {{ill|lt=earthenware|Q1377111|WD=Q1377111|1=Earthenware}} has been excavated from Myōjin-yama north of the shrine.

According to tradition, the origin is that on Kōki 4 (spring, February 23), {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} worshiped the {{ill|lt=Imperial ancestral deities|Q11581090|WD=Q11581090|lt=imperial heavenly ancestors|1=Ancestors of the Emperor}} (or {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}) at this place. Later, the Tomi-no-Muraji clan enshrined Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and his consort in this area where their ancestor had resided. Including smaller shrines, 22 deities are worshiped<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://ntomishrine.wixsite.com/official |title=Tomi Shrine |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref>.

=== Enshrined deities ===
The honden faces south, with western and eastern honden side by side. Deities of the western honden are {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3275901|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Kamimusubi}}, Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto, and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}; deities of the eastern honden are {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Honda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto is also called Tomi Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and Kijima Daimyōjin.

=== Cultural properties ===
*On April 3, Reiwa 2, the honden, haiden, shinsenjo, chōzuya, and shrine office were registered as national tangible cultural properties by the Agency for Cultural Affairs.
*{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kayu uranai|1=UNKNOWN}} was designated an intangible folk cultural property of Nara City on March 1, Shōwa 57.
=== Precinct shrines ===
*Haraedo Shrine (deities: {{ill|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Kami}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hayakitsuhime-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Ibukido-nushi-no-Kami|Q86734190|WD=Q86734190|1=Ibukidonushi no Kami}}, {{ill|lt=Hayasasurahime-no-Kami|Q136731213|WD=Q136731213|1=Hayasasurahime}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Uwatsutsuno-o-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakatsutsuno-o-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sokotsutsuno-o-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}})
*Yamamuro Shrine (deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|1=Ōmononushi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sugawara no Michizane-kō|1=UNKNOWN}})
*Toyoho Shrine (deities: {{ill|lt=Ōhirume-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōhirume-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Toyoukehime-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyoukehime-no-Kami}}, Ame-no-Uzume-no-Kami)
*Kōjin Shrine (deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami|1=Ōyamatsumi}}, Niwatakatsuhi-no-Kami)
*Hirata Shrine (deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Kami|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami|WD=Q3090037}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yae-Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}})

=== Access ===
*2 km west from {{ill|lt=Kujō Station (Nara)|Q6442373|WD=Q6442373|lt=Kujō Station|1=Kujō Station (Nara)}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Kashihara Line|Q2491066|WD=Q2491066|1=Kashihara Line}} (about 20 minutes on foot)

*Parking available

'''Tomi Shrine''' (とみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=Engishiki Jinmeichō |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref> {{ill|lt=enshrined|Q927365|WD=Q927365|1=Louis de Bourbon, Duke of Montpensier}} in Ishiki-chō, {{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134|1=Nara (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} (ranked in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 14). Ritual {{ill|lt=earthenware|Q1377111|WD=Q1377111|1=Earthenware}} has been excavated from Myōjin-yama north of the shrine.

According to tradition, the origin is that on Kōki 4 (spring, February 23), {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} worshiped the {{ill|lt=Imperial ancestral deities|Q11581090|WD=Q11581090|lt=imperial heavenly ancestors|1=Ancestors of the Emperor}} (or {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}) at this place. Later, the Tomi-no-Muraji clan enshrined Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and his consort in this area where their ancestor had resided. Including smaller shrines, 22 deities are worshiped<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://ntomishrine.wixsite.com/official |title=Tomi Shrine |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref>.

=== Enshrined deities ===
The honden faces south, with western and eastern honden side by side. Deities of the western honden are {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3275901|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Kamimusubi}}, Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto, and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}; deities of the eastern honden are {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Honda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto is also called Tomi Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and Kijima Daimyōjin.

=== Cultural properties ===
*On April 3, Reiwa 2, the honden, haiden, shinsenjo, chōzuya, and shrine office were registered as national tangible cultural properties by the Agency for Cultural Affairs.
*{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kayu uranai|1=UNKNOWN}} was designated an intangible folk cultural property of Nara City on March 1, Shōwa 57.
=== Precinct shrines ===
*Haraedo Shrine (deities: {{ill|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Kami}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hayakitsuhime-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Ibukido-nushi-no-Kami|Q86734190|WD=Q86734190|1=Ibukidonushi no Kami}}, {{ill|lt=Hayasasurahime-no-Kami|Q136731213|WD=Q136731213|1=Hayasasurahime}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Uwatsutsuno-o-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakatsutsuno-o-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sokotsutsuno-o-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}})
*Yamamuro Shrine (deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|1=Ōmononushi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sugawara no Michizane-kō|1=UNKNOWN}})
*Toyoho Shrine (deities: {{ill|lt=Ōhirume-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōhirume-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Toyoukehime-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyoukehime-no-Kami}}, Ame-no-Uzume-no-Kami)
*Kōjin Shrine (deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami|1=Ōyamatsumi}}, Niwatakatsuhi-no-Kami)
*Hirata Shrine (deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Kami|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami|WD=Q3090037}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yae-Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}})

=== Access ===
*2 km west from {{ill|lt=Kujō Station (Nara)|Q6442373|WD=Q6442373|lt=Kujō Station|1=Kujō Station (Nara)}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Kashihara Line|Q2491066|WD=Q2491066|1=Kashihara Line}} (about 20 minutes on foot)

*Parking available

{{nihongo|'''Tomi Shrine'''|登彌神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

== Japanese content ==

'''Tomi Shrine''' (とみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=Engishiki Jinmeichō |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref> {{ill|lt=enshrined|Q927365|WD=Q927365|1=Louis de Bourbon, Duke of Montpensier}} in Ishiki-chō, {{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134|1=Nara (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} (ranked in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 14). Ritual {{ill|lt=earthenware|Q1377111|WD=Q1377111|1=Earthenware}} has been excavated from Myōjin-yama north of the shrine.

According to tradition, the origin is that on Kōki 4 (spring, February 23), {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} worshiped the {{ill|lt=Imperial ancestral deities|Q11581090|WD=Q11581090|lt=imperial heavenly ancestors|1=Ancestors of the Emperor}} (or {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}) at this place. Later, the Tomi-no-Muraji clan enshrined Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and his consort in this area where their ancestor had resided. Including smaller shrines, 22 deities are worshiped<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://ntomishrine.wixsite.com/official |title=Tomi Shrine |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref>.

=== Enshrined deities ===
The honden faces south, with western and eastern honden side by side. Deities of the western honden are {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3275901|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Kamimusubi}}, Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto, and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}; deities of the eastern honden are {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Honda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto is also called Tomi Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and Kijima Daimyōjin.

=== Cultural properties ===
*On April 3, Reiwa 2, the honden, haiden, shinsenjo, chōzuya, and shrine office were registered as national tangible cultural properties by the Agency for Cultural Affairs.
*{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kayu uranai|1=UNKNOWN}} was designated an intangible folk cultural property of Nara City on March 1, Shōwa 57.
=== Precinct shrines ===
*Haraedo Shrine (deities: {{ill|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Kami}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hayakitsuhime-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Ibukido-nushi-no-Kami|Q86734190|WD=Q86734190|1=Ibukidonushi no Kami}}, {{ill|lt=Hayasasurahime-no-Kami|Q136731213|WD=Q136731213|1=Hayasasurahime}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Uwatsutsuno-o-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakatsutsuno-o-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sokotsutsuno-o-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}})
*Yamamuro Shrine (deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|1=Ōmononushi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sugawara no Michizane-kō|1=UNKNOWN}})
*Toyoho Shrine (deities: {{ill|lt=Ōhirume-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōhirume-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Toyoukehime-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyoukehime-no-Kami}}, Ame-no-Uzume-no-Kami)
*Kōjin Shrine (deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami|1=Ōyamatsumi}}, Niwatakatsuhi-no-Kami)
*Hirata Shrine (deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Kami|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami|WD=Q3090037}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yae-Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}})

=== Access ===
*2 km west from {{ill|lt=Kujō Station (Nara)|Q6442373|WD=Q6442373|lt=Kujō Station|1=Kujō Station (Nara)}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Kashihara Line|Q2491066|WD=Q2491066|1=Kashihara Line}} (about 20 minutes on foot)

*Parking available

{{nihongo|'''Tomi Shrine'''|登彌神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

'''Tomi Shrine''' (とみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=Engishiki Jinmeichō |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref> {{ill|lt=enshrined|Q927365|WD=Q927365|1=Louis de Bourbon, Duke of Montpensier}} in Ishiki-chō, {{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134|1=Nara (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} (ranked in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 14). Ritual {{ill|lt=earthenware|Q1377111|WD=Q1377111|1=Earthenware}} has been excavated from Myōjin-yama north of the shrine.

According to tradition, the origin is that on Kōki 4 (spring, February 23), {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} worshiped the {{ill|lt=Imperial ancestral deities|Q11581090|WD=Q11581090|lt=imperial heavenly ancestors|1=Ancestors of the Emperor}} (or {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}) at this place. Later, the Tomi-no-Muraji clan enshrined Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and his consort in this area where their ancestor had resided. Including smaller shrines, 22 deities are worshiped<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://ntomishrine.wixsite.com/official |title=Tomi Shrine |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref>.

=== Enshrined deities ===
The honden faces south, with western and eastern honden side by side. Deities of the western honden are {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3275901|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Kamimusubi}}, Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto, and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}; deities of the eastern honden are {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Honda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto is also called Tomi Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and Kijima Daimyōjin.

=== Cultural properties ===
*On April 3, Reiwa 2, the honden, haiden, shinsenjo, chōzuya, and shrine office were registered as national tangible cultural properties by the Agency for Cultural Affairs.
*{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kayu uranai|1=UNKNOWN}} was designated an intangible folk cultural property of Nara City on March 1, Shōwa 57.
=== Precinct shrines ===
*Haraedo Shrine (deities: {{ill|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Kami}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hayakitsuhime-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Ibukido-nushi-no-Kami|Q86734190|WD=Q86734190|1=Ibukidonushi no Kami}}, {{ill|lt=Hayasasurahime-no-Kami|Q136731213|WD=Q136731213|1=Hayasasurahime}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Uwatsutsuno-o-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakatsutsuno-o-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sokotsutsuno-o-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}})
*Yamamuro Shrine (deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|1=Ōmononushi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sugawara no Michizane-kō|1=UNKNOWN}})
*Toyoho Shrine (deities: {{ill|lt=Ōhirume-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōhirume-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Toyoukehime-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyoukehime-no-Kami}}, Ame-no-Uzume-no-Kami)
*Kōjin Shrine (deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami|1=Ōyamatsumi}}, Niwatakatsuhi-no-Kami)
*Hirata Shrine (deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Kami|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami|WD=Q3090037}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yae-Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}})

=== Access ===
*2 km west from {{ill|lt=Kujō Station (Nara)|Q6442373|WD=Q6442373|lt=Kujō Station|1=Kujō Station (Nara)}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Kashihara Line|Q2491066|WD=Q2491066|1=Kashihara Line}} (about 20 minutes on foot)

*Parking available

{{nihongo|'''Tomi Shrine'''|登彌神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

== Japanese content ==
{{混同|For the shrine in Sakurai, Nara|{{ill|lt=Tomi Shrine|Q11602724|WD=Q11602724|1=Tomi Shrine (Q11602724)}}}}

'''Tomi Shrine''' (とみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=Engishiki Jinmeichō |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref> {{ill|lt=enshrined|Q927365|WD=Q927365|1=Louis de Bourbon, Duke of Montpensier}} in Ishiki-chō, {{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134|1=Nara (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} (ranked in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 14). Ritual {{ill|lt=earthenware|Q1377111|WD=Q1377111|1=Earthenware}} has been excavated from Myōjin-yama north of the shrine.

According to tradition, the origin is that on Kōki 4 (spring, February 23), {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} worshiped the {{ill|lt=Imperial ancestral deities|Q11581090|WD=Q11581090|lt=imperial heavenly ancestors|1=Ancestors of the Emperor}} (or {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}) at this place. Later, the Tomi-no-Muraji clan enshrined Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and his consort in this area where their ancestor had resided. Including smaller shrines, 22 deities are worshiped<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://ntomishrine.wixsite.com/official |title=Tomi Shrine |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref>.

=== Enshrined deities ===
The honden faces south, with western and eastern honden side by side. Deities of the western honden are {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3275901|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Kamimusubi}}, Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto, and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}; deities of the eastern honden are {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Honda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto is also called Tomi Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and Kijima Daimyōjin.

=== Cultural properties ===
*On April 3, Reiwa 2, the honden, haiden, shinsenjo, chōzuya, and shrine office were registered as national tangible cultural properties by the Agency for Cultural Affairs.
*{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kayu uranai|1=UNKNOWN}} was designated an intangible folk cultural property of Nara City on March 1, Shōwa 57.
=== Precinct shrines ===
*Haraedo Shrine (deities: {{ill|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Kami}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hayakitsuhime-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Ibukido-nushi-no-Kami|Q86734190|WD=Q86734190|1=Ibukidonushi no Kami}}, {{ill|lt=Hayasasurahime-no-Kami|Q136731213|WD=Q136731213|1=Hayasasurahime}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Uwatsutsuno-o-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakatsutsuno-o-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sokotsutsuno-o-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}})
*Yamamuro Shrine (deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|1=Ōmononushi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sugawara no Michizane-kō|1=UNKNOWN}})
*Toyoho Shrine (deities: {{ill|lt=Ōhirume-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōhirume-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Toyoukehime-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyoukehime-no-Kami}}, Ame-no-Uzume-no-Kami)
*Kōjin Shrine (deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami|1=Ōyamatsumi}}, Niwatakatsuhi-no-Kami)
*Hirata Shrine (deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Kami|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami|WD=Q3090037}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yae-Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}})

=== Access ===
*2 km west from {{ill|lt=Kujō Station (Nara)|Q6442373|WD=Q6442373|lt=Kujō Station|1=Kujō Station (Nara)}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Kashihara Line|Q2491066|WD=Q2491066|1=Kashihara Line}} (about 20 minutes on foot)

*Parking available

'''Tomi Shrine''' (とみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=Engishiki Jinmeichō |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref> {{ill|lt=enshrined|Q927365|WD=Q927365|1=Louis de Bourbon, Duke of Montpensier}} in Ishiki-chō, {{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134|1=Nara (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} (ranked in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 14). Ritual {{ill|lt=earthenware|Q1377111|WD=Q1377111|1=Earthenware}} has been excavated from Myōjin-yama north of the shrine.

According to tradition, the origin is that on Kōki 4 (spring, February 23), {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} worshiped the {{ill|lt=Imperial ancestral deities|Q11581090|WD=Q11581090|lt=imperial heavenly ancestors|1=Ancestors of the Emperor}} (or {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}) at this place. Later, the Tomi-no-Muraji clan enshrined Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and his consort in this area where their ancestor had resided. Including smaller shrines, 22 deities are worshiped<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://ntomishrine.wixsite.com/official |title=Tomi Shrine |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref>.

=== Enshrined deities ===
The honden faces south, with western and eastern honden side by side. Deities of the western honden are {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3275901|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Kamimusubi}}, Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto, and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}; deities of the eastern honden are {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Honda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto is also called Tomi Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and Kijima Daimyōjin.

=== Cultural properties ===
*On April 3, Reiwa 2, the honden, haiden, shinsenjo, chōzuya, and shrine office were registered as national tangible cultural properties by the Agency for Cultural Affairs.
*{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kayu uranai|1=UNKNOWN}} was designated an intangible folk cultural property of Nara City on March 1, Shōwa 57.
=== Precinct shrines ===
*Haraedo Shrine (deities: {{ill|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Kami}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hayakitsuhime-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Ibukido-nushi-no-Kami|Q86734190|WD=Q86734190|1=Ibukidonushi no Kami}}, {{ill|lt=Hayasasurahime-no-Kami|Q136731213|WD=Q136731213|1=Hayasasurahime}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Uwatsutsuno-o-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakatsutsuno-o-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sokotsutsuno-o-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}})
*Yamamuro Shrine (deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|1=Ōmononushi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sugawara no Michizane-kō|1=UNKNOWN}})
*Toyoho Shrine (deities: {{ill|lt=Ōhirume-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōhirume-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Toyoukehime-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyoukehime-no-Kami}}, Ame-no-Uzume-no-Kami)
*Kōjin Shrine (deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami|1=Ōyamatsumi}}, Niwatakatsuhi-no-Kami)
*Hirata Shrine (deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Kami|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami|WD=Q3090037}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yae-Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}})

=== Access ===
*2 km west from {{ill|lt=Kujō Station (Nara)|Q6442373|WD=Q6442373|lt=Kujō Station|1=Kujō Station (Nara)}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Kashihara Line|Q2491066|WD=Q2491066|1=Kashihara Line}} (about 20 minutes on foot)

*Parking available

{{nihongo|'''Tomi Shrine'''|登彌神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

'''Tomi Shrine''' (とみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=Engishiki Jinmeichō |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref> {{ill|lt=enshrined|Q927365|WD=Q927365|1=Louis de Bourbon, Duke of Montpensier}} in Ishiki-chō, {{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134|1=Nara (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} (ranked in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 14). Ritual {{ill|lt=earthenware|Q1377111|WD=Q1377111|1=Earthenware}} has been excavated from Myōjin-yama north of the shrine.

According to tradition, the origin is that on Kōki 4 (spring, February 23), {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} worshiped the {{ill|lt=Imperial ancestral deities|Q11581090|WD=Q11581090|lt=imperial heavenly ancestors|1=Ancestors of the Emperor}} (or {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}) at this place. Later, the Tomi-no-Muraji clan enshrined Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and his consort in this area where their ancestor had resided. Including smaller shrines, 22 deities are worshiped<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://ntomishrine.wixsite.com/official |title=Tomi Shrine |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref>.

=== Enshrined deities ===
The honden faces south, with western and eastern honden side by side. Deities of the western honden are {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3275901|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Kamimusubi}}, Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto, and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}; deities of the eastern honden are {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Honda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto is also called Tomi Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and Kijima Daimyōjin.

=== Cultural properties ===
*On April 3, Reiwa 2, the honden, haiden, shinsenjo, chōzuya, and shrine office were registered as national tangible cultural properties by the Agency for Cultural Affairs.
*{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kayu uranai|1=UNKNOWN}} was designated an intangible folk cultural property of Nara City on March 1, Shōwa 57.
=== Precinct shrines ===
*Haraedo Shrine (deities: {{ill|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Kami}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hayakitsuhime-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Ibukido-nushi-no-Kami|Q86734190|WD=Q86734190|1=Ibukidonushi no Kami}}, {{ill|lt=Hayasasurahime-no-Kami|Q136731213|WD=Q136731213|1=Hayasasurahime}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Uwatsutsuno-o-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakatsutsuno-o-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sokotsutsuno-o-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}})
*Yamamuro Shrine (deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|1=Ōmononushi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sugawara no Michizane-kō|1=UNKNOWN}})
*Toyoho Shrine (deities: {{ill|lt=Ōhirume-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōhirume-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Toyoukehime-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyoukehime-no-Kami}}, Ame-no-Uzume-no-Kami)
*Kōjin Shrine (deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami|1=Ōyamatsumi}}, Niwatakatsuhi-no-Kami)
*Hirata Shrine (deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Kami|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami|WD=Q3090037}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yae-Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}})

=== Access ===
*2 km west from {{ill|lt=Kujō Station (Nara)|Q6442373|WD=Q6442373|lt=Kujō Station|1=Kujō Station (Nara)}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Kashihara Line|Q2491066|WD=Q2491066|1=Kashihara Line}} (about 20 minutes on foot)

*Parking available

{{nihongo|'''Tomi Shrine'''|登彌神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].

'''Tomi Shrine''' (とみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |title=Engishiki Jinmeichō |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref> {{ill|lt=enshrined|Q927365|WD=Q927365|1=Louis de Bourbon, Duke of Montpensier}} in Ishiki-chō, {{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134|1=Nara (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} (ranked in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 14). Ritual {{ill|lt=earthenware|Q1377111|WD=Q1377111|1=Earthenware}} has been excavated from Myōjin-yama north of the shrine.

According to tradition, the origin is that on Kōki 4 (spring, February 23), {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} worshiped the {{ill|lt=Imperial ancestral deities|Q11581090|WD=Q11581090|lt=imperial heavenly ancestors|1=Ancestors of the Emperor}} (or {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}) at this place. Later, the Tomi-no-Muraji clan enshrined Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and his consort in this area where their ancestor had resided. Including smaller shrines, 22 deities are worshiped<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://ntomishrine.wixsite.com/official |title=Tomi Shrine |access-date=2024-5-24}}</ref>.

=== Enshrined deities ===
The honden faces south, with western and eastern honden side by side. Deities of the western honden are {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3275901|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Kamimusubi}}, Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto, and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}; deities of the eastern honden are {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Honda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto is also called Tomi Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto and Kijima Daimyōjin.

=== Cultural properties ===
*On April 3, Reiwa 2, the honden, haiden, shinsenjo, chōzuya, and shrine office were registered as national tangible cultural properties by the Agency for Cultural Affairs.
*{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kayu uranai|1=UNKNOWN}} was designated an intangible folk cultural property of Nara City on March 1, Shōwa 57.
=== Precinct shrines ===
*Haraedo Shrine (deities: {{ill|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Kami}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hayakitsuhime-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Ibukido-nushi-no-Kami|Q86734190|WD=Q86734190|1=Ibukidonushi no Kami}}, {{ill|lt=Hayasasurahime-no-Kami|Q136731213|WD=Q136731213|1=Hayasasurahime}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Uwatsutsuno-o-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakatsutsuno-o-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sokotsutsuno-o-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}})
*Yamamuro Shrine (deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|1=Ōmononushi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sugawara no Michizane-kō|1=UNKNOWN}})
*Toyoho Shrine (deities: {{ill|lt=Ōhirume-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōhirume-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Toyoukehime-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyoukehime-no-Kami}}, Ame-no-Uzume-no-Kami)
*Kōjin Shrine (deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami|1=Ōyamatsumi}}, Niwatakatsuhi-no-Kami)
*Hirata Shrine (deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Kami|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami|WD=Q3090037}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yae-Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}})

=== Access ===
*2 km west from {{ill|lt=Kujō Station (Nara)|Q6442373|WD=Q6442373|lt=Kujō Station|1=Kujō Station (Nara)}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Kashihara Line|Q2491066|WD=Q2491066|1=Kashihara Line}} (about 20 minutes on foot)

*Parking available
